docs.title: 图斯克地区常用姓名考
docs.text: 图斯克地区的命名文化与其复杂的语言历史、地理分布及社会阶层紧密相关。从海峡沿岸的帝国核心区，到西部山地的咕洛部落，再到诺瓦亚海沿岸的古拉普里奥城邦，不同的文化圈呈现出截然不同的命名逻辑。本条目旨在梳理这些区域的常用姓名及其背后的词源与文化含义。

docs.title: 1 图斯克帝国
docs.text: 作为大陆的主导文明，海峡图斯克语 (truskil mielail）的命名规则体现了严谨的阶级划分与分析语特征。贵族与精英阶层倾向于使用结构复杂的复合词，以彰显家族的历史渊源或道德追求；而平民名字则多具描述性，常与自然景物或出生地相关。

docs.title: 1.1 贵族姓氏 (naamar)
docs.text:
图斯克贵族的姓氏通常采用古雅的复合词结构，或是保留了拉埃拉德原始语的痕迹。在构词法上，贵族姓氏常采用宾语前置（OV）或修饰语前置的结构，这在以SVO为主的图斯克语中是一种庄重、概念化的体现。

皇室与军事贵族 帝国最显赫的姓氏往往与统治权、战争及守护有关。

  • tyesuku（铁苏库）：tyesu “暖”, ku “石”，综合起来意为“暖石”，或指向铁苏库家族早期的职业，即铁匠

  • moswarike（莫斯瓦里克）：意为“骑手”（moswari 骑 + ke 从事……的人），是最早入驻图斯克的咕洛部族。

  • turhaike（图尔海克）：意为“战士”或“尚武者”（turhai 战斗 + ke 者），象征勇武的战斗精神，是极具代表性的武勋世家大姓，是莫斯瓦里克图斯克化的分家之一。

  • jaujia（焦吉亚）：意为“荣耀卫士”（jaujia 褚，即护卫）

  • kuinsyec（奎恩谢克）：意为“黄金剑”或“王之剑”，奎列奥王朝时代流传下来的贵族名

  • laigrakou（莱格拉寇）：意为“大君”或“领袖”（gai 大 + grakou 夫/主），源自部落时代的首领称谓。

神权与古老血统 部分姓氏源自创世神话或自然崇拜，象征家族拥有古老的祭祀权或通灵能力。

  • heimarit（海玛利特）：意为“日赐”或“天恩”（heim 日 + arit 给），指家族先祖有受命于天的传说。

  • tauswi（陶斯威）：意为“天灵”（tau 天 + swi 灵），带有萨满或通灵色彩的古老姓氏。

  • hambwasia（汉布瓦西亚）：意为“神话”或“史诗”，通常是负责记录历史的史官家族。

  • hetuke（赫图克）：意为“夜行者”（hetu 夜 + -ke 者），可能与藏海神信仰或守夜职责有关。

  • numsib（努昔卜）：意为“千叶”（numrea 千 + Sib 叶），源自翻海宗的宗教概念，象征繁荣与无穷变数。

行政与财富 随着帝国商业与官僚体系的发展，许多姓氏体现了财富、领地与行政职能。

  • ruilansom（瑞兰索姆）：意为“多财者”或“富足”（ruilan 财产、丰饶 + som 形容词化），黑蔑特城周早期的开发者家族之一，在波拉斯蓬麾下进攻南方拉普里奥人矿区，矿区被分封给该家族，并改名为 ruilian，其家族名遂成 ruilansom。

  • goiraudun（戈劳盾）：意为“宏殿”（goirau 殿 + -dun 指大），是古代图斯克的工匠家族之一

  • nowdun（诺顿）：意为“弘海”或，诺瓦亚海沿岸常见姓氏，没有大的宗族，大多是各港口的小城市贵族。

  • eratuku（埃拉图库）：意为“地主”或“领主”（erat 地 + uku 主），图斯克向各地殖民的过程中，由帝国中央派往管理各地的一批官员的职位，后演化成姓氏。

  • wisuku（维苏库）：意为“林主”，类似埃拉图库，但主要领地多为林地。

  • guduke（古督克）：意为“缝补者”或“储藏”（gudu 缝 + -ke 者），源自手工艺者或管理仓库的官员。

品质 许多贵族以抽象的美德或愿景作为姓氏，标榜家族的道德高度。

  • lousan（洛萨安）：意为“正道”或“善途”（Lous 道 + An 正），据考也可能是设计马塔肖萨门大道的家族改名而成

  • porlasom（波拉斯索姆）：意为“古直”或“守旧”（Por 旧 + las 直），象征古老、正直与对传统的坚守。

  • ilesait（伊勒赛特）：意为“辉煌”或“光耀”，古老的家族，古代盛产女祭司的家族

  • giateis（吉瓦泰斯）：意为“秘识”或“藏智”（gia 藏 + teis 知），象征掌握不为人知的知识。

  • norkosom（诺科索姆）：意为“多子的”，寄托着希望家族多子嗣的愿望。

  • anioksait（阿尼奥克赛特）：意为“周全”或“轮回”（aniok 转），表达圆满之意。

  • ludimart（鲁迪马特）：意为“乐土”或“欢聚地”（ludi 玩 + -mart 场所），象征愉悦与和谐。

  • drodlaisom（德罗德莱索姆）：意为“深思”（drod 心 + lais 长+-som 形容词化后缀），象征性格稳重。

  • aritsom（阿利特索姆）：象征乐善好施与信守承诺。

  • aseasait（阿塞亚赛特）：意为“非凡”，象征与众不同的家族气质。

  • lasjodrod（莱西奥德罗）：意为“阔心”（laisiok 宽 + drod 心），象征包容与大度。

自然图腾 部分家族以自然界的事物为姓，体现了图斯克人与环境的连接。

  • seitwise（塞特维斯）：意为“果树”或“李”（Seit 果 + Wise 树），以植物图腾为姓，象征生命力。

  • basib（巴希布）：意为“水草”或“菰”（Ba 水 + Sib 叶），常见于水乡家族。

  • barol（巴罗尔）：意为“深水”或“沉”（Ba 水 + Rol 深），象征深邃沉静。

  • laiswise（莱斯维斯）：意为“高树”或“杨”（Lais 长 + Wise 树），象征挺拔。

  • samemgreke（萨梅姆格雷克）：意为“山民”或“守山人”，象征山地居民的坚韧。

  • betusait（贝图赛特）：意为“寒冷”或“凛”（Betu 冷），多源自俺东北方移民。

docs.title: 1.2 贵族名字 (Namo)
docs.text:
贵族的名字更注重个人的品质、愿景，常使用动词派生或名词与形容词的组合。

帝王与领袖气质

  • laiswi（莱斯卫）：意为“宏灵”或“大气”（Lai 大 + Swi 灵），帝王之名，象征精神力量的宏大。

  • teiskwa（泰斯夸）：意为“闻名”或“被知者”（Teis 知 + -kwa 被动），希望孩子名扬天下。

  • yingukho（因古霍）：意为“必胜”（Ying 必 + Gukho 胜），表达对胜利的绝对信心。

  • oleman（奥勒曼）：意为“善果”或“成就”（Olem 结果 + An 好），希望人生有好的结局。

尚武与行动力

  • kansoi（坎索伊）：意为“快剑”或“瞬斩”（Kan 剑 + -soi 急速），希望孩子剑术超群。

  • jeposoi（耶普索伊）：意为“疾行”或“冲锋”（Jepo 走 + -soi 急速），象征行动力与迅捷。

  • jaujyaan（焦加安）：意为“忠卫”（Jaujya 卫士 + An 好），寓意成为优秀的守护者。

  • guit（古特巴古）：意为“用石头”，来自古老的投石兵。

  • gudun（古盾）：意为“巨石”（Gu 石 + -dun 指大），象征性格坚毅。

  • wetlai（维特莱）：意为“大风”或“狂风”，象征行事雷厉风行。

  • wetpo（韦伯）：意为“风化”，象征行事像风一样迅捷，但较前一个名字轻盈。

智慧、精神与美德

  • Drodaucy（德罗道奇）：意为“静心”（Drod 心 + Aucy 静），寓意性格沉稳、处变不惊。

  • Ningswi（宁斯威）：意为“能灵”或“灵能”，象征拥有强大的精神潜能。

  • Tyaan（提亚安）：意为“善愿”或“好意”（Tyaa 意愿 + An 好），象征心存善念。

  • Azozi（阿佐齐）：意为“无畏”（A- 无 + Zozi 怕），否定派生名，希望孩子勇敢。

  • Porteis（波特伊斯）：意为“先知”或“预见”，常用于学者或祭司。

  • Porteiske（波特伊斯克）：意为“大巫”或“预言家”，指使预言成真的人。

  • Soryaz（索里亚兹）：意为“织造者”（Sori 织 + -yaz 态），象征具有创造力，能构建秩序。

  • Troleug（特罗列格）：意为“燧人”或“造火”，象征智慧与开创。

  • Dayaud（德奥德）：意为“救赎”或“续命”（De- 使 + A-ud 不死），递归派生词，常用于医生。

  • Ansait（安赛特）：意为“安康”或“平安”，最朴素美好的祝福。

自然与美貌

  • wespan（韦斯帕安）：意为“绝色”或“亮彩”（Wespa 色 + an 好的、美的），形容容貌出众。

  • Sniowan（斯尼奥万）：意为“悦目”或“俊美”，形容令人赏心悦目。

  • Heimbor（海姆博）：意为“旭日”（Heim 日 + -bor 起始），象征新生与希望。

  • Rigujiu（里古久）：意为“白雪”，象征品行高洁。

  • Bafara（巴法拉）：意为“流水”，象征生命顺遂。

  • Baik（拜克）：意为“似水”或“柔顺”，性格温润。

  • Weten（韦腾）：意为“风化”或“随风”，象征自由洒脱。

docs.title: 2 咕洛部落
docs.text:
咕洛语属于黏着语，其姓名风格粗犷，常与猛兽、自然力相关。

氏族名

  • Khenxed（肯赫德）：意为“黑狼”，勇猛好战的部族。

  • Olaleuk（奥拉列克）：意为“火头”或“炎首”，崇拜火神的部族。

  • Mörikesep（莫里克瑟普）：意为“高马”，善于骑射的部族，莫斯瓦里克分家（语音流变）。

  • Rigurchi（里古尔奇）：意为“雪众”或“雪族”，居住在极北冰原的部族。

个人名

  • Vesnö-sar（维斯诺萨）：意为“猎手”，职业专家名。

  • Bordas-kim（博尔达斯基姆）：意为“行直者”或“正直”。

  • Gok-ola（格克奥拉）：意为“铁头”或“天首”。

  • Joluk-xar（乔鲁克哈）：意为“暴怒者”。

  • Ila-sora（伊拉索拉）：意为“多光”或“璀璨”。

  • Taamek（塔梅克）：意为“天客”，也作为问候语“你好”。

docs.title: 3 古拉普里奥城邦
docs.text:
古拉普里奥语受海洋贸易影响，具有流利的双元音和屈折语特征，并发展出了基于“俗世/自然”的性别冠词系统（Le/La）。

家族/商会名 通常使用 La（远性/自然性）开头的词，表示家族源远流长。

  • La Valau（拉·瓦劳）：意为“海家族”，垄断航运的巨头。

  • La Kuoju（拉·库尤）：意为“银家族”，银行家或银矿主。

  • La Hura（拉·胡拉）：意为“时家族”，古老的钟表匠或预言家。

  • La Sedia（拉·塞迪亚）：意为“森家族”，木材商人。

个人名 男性或世俗职业多用 Le 类（近性），女性或神职多用 La 类（远性）。

  • Arino（阿里诺）：Le类名，意为“商人”或“贾”。

  • Lauro（劳罗）：Le类名，意为“伟岸者”。

  • Foko（福科）：Le类名，意为“飞翔者”，常指信使。

  • Vala（瓦拉）：La类名，意为“海女”。

  • Maura（莫拉）：La类名，意为“云”或“雾”。

  • Leuga（列加）：La类名，意为“烈火”，指热情或红发的女性。

docs.title: 图斯克语
docs.text:
Abstract

本书是对图斯克语的详细描述，主要参考了黑蔑特地区的海峡图斯克语，也即图斯克元一百七十五年前后，铁苏库·莱斯卫laiswi在位的第二年，帝国所采用的官方语言。这本书的成书要感谢铁苏库王朝的第一位皇帝，也即铁苏库·德卓黑三世，他在灵语的启示下，他发掘了一些古老的遗迹，位于米斯特那提拉境内的古咕洛语石碑、拉普里奥山区的密比恩方尖碑、古图斯克的许多雕像都是在那时发现的；他随后设立了皇家学会来对帝国的一切进行研究，在帝国省部中划出一个机构专门为学会的研究提供资金和人力资源支持。他的执政持续了十五年，在这期间，学会对前文所提及的各种遗迹进行了进一步的研究，试图破译其上的碑文，这一部分构成了今天图斯克人对古图斯克语、乃至原始拉埃拉德语的部分认识。德卓黑三世死亡后，学会的经费被削减，对古迹的研究放缓，在人手欠缺的情况下，学会对已有的资料进行了整理，并且根据对各种语言的比较，开始研究图斯克各地区的语言，这也是本书成书的基础之一。今上莱斯卫登基之后，决定修一些记录图斯克各地情况的书籍，恰逢大皎忽匝依在试图拓宽对灵语的研究，于是便委托协会撰写这本关于图斯克语的书籍，来佐助两者的研究。

根据学会之前的研究以及整理。包括黑蔑特、铁苏库、图尔哈、俺东、以及大拉普里奥的广袤图斯克语地区，共同构成一个方言连续体。从大的分布上来说，可以分成海峡方言（沿海方言）和山地方言，而山地方言又分成东部和西部。

沿着诺瓦亚海的一圈是沿海方言，这一方言包括了[[黑蔑特方言]]在内，这一方言在用词上呈现出惊人的高度统一，但是在发音音段上有一些区别。西部的山地方言包括了北起俺东，南至齐米亚克的广袤山地地区，形成一个相对连贯的连续体。东部的山地方言又分做南北两大块，靠近咕洛族的北部山区受到很多咕洛语的影响，在发音音段上和用词上都相对特别，而南部方言则是和当地一些山地居民的土著语言进行了融合，有许多独特的用词和语法现象。

本书中如果仅提到图斯克语，一般指代的即是帝国目前所通行的官方语言海峡图斯克语，也即黑蔑特方言。读者应当认识到的是，根据学会之前的记载，图斯克帝国中非图斯克族群主导的地区，大多实行双语制：拉普里奥地区的原始语言作为弱势语言在帝国长期推行图斯克语的政策下已经逐渐式微，当代的拉普里奥语更类似一种图斯克语的方言；咕洛族地区，人们实行较为严格的双语制，一方面是因为本地守旧派抗拒图斯克化，另一方面是咕洛各部族之间的联系本就不像图斯克的各城之间那样紧密，本身就存在相对孤立的方言演化。简而言之，如果你会说黑蔑特的海峡图斯克语，在帝国境内较为富庶的，人口相对密集的地区还是能畅行无阻的，尽管你可能在拉普里奥地区以及东南的山区碰到一些麻烦，但是在咕洛地区的城镇地带以及西北的俺东、八股纳洛等地你都能找到熟练使用官方语言的向导或旅店。

译者注：本书经过译制，许多地方的视角可能会比较奇怪，所以在接下来的段落中未必完全按照作者原意翻译，会结合一些以现代汉语为视角的注解，例如词间对照翻译和中译就是使读者能够快速上手图斯克语的一个常见段落。

docs.title: 1.1 音段总藏
docs.text:
图斯克语的音系颇为简单：

标准图斯克语基础的元音只有五个音段，其中两个 a、o 存在鼻化变体，但在部分方言中 i, e 可能也存在在鼻辅音附近的鼻化现象；存在大量双元音，尽管存在绝对音位上的差距，但是仍可以视作两个元音音位之间的移动。

标准图斯克语的辅音音段共有 24 个，如下表所注。总体而言较为对称, 存在较为规律的送气不送气对立，但缺乏浊塞音；龈颚音的一列实际上是齿龈音在前元音 i 前的懒化，在正字法中不表达。

docs.title: 1.1.1 元音
docs.text:
下表是图斯克语的元音拉丁正字及其 IPA 对照

元音 发音（IPA） a /ä/ o /ɔ/ e /ɛ/ i /i/ u /u/ aa /ã/ oo /ɔ̃/ ai /ai/ oi /ɔi/ au /aʊ/ ia /ia/ iu /iu/ ui /ui/ ua /ua/ ue /uɛ/

docs.title: 1.1.2 辅音
docs.text: 序号 拉丁正字 IPA 1 m m 2 n n 3 ng ŋ 4 b p 5 d t 6 g k 7 p pʰ 8 t tʰ 9 k kʰ 10 h h 11 s s,ɕ 12 z z,ʑ 13 j ts,tɕ 14 c tsʰ,tɕʰ 15 l l 16 r r 17 w w 18 y j 发音部位 送气 唇音 齿龈音 龈颚音 软腭音 声门音 鼻音 m n ŋ(ng) 塞音 不送气 p (b) t (d) k (g) 送气 pʰ (p) tʰ(t) kʰ(k) 擦音 s ɕ (s) h 浊 z ʑ (z) 塞擦音 不送气 ts (j) tɕ (j) 送气 tsʰ (c) tɕʰ (c) 浊 dz dʑ (dz) 流音 l~ɾ(l) 颤音 r (r) 边音 w j (y)

docs.title: 1.2 语音配列
docs.text: 在词语派生，变形的过程中，可能会发生各种音位的连缀，这里对图斯克语中常见的情况进行简单的叙述。

docs.title: 1.2.1 元音连缀
docs.text: 图斯克语的元音连缀规则很简单： 两个短元音连缀时，若存在对应双元音，则读作对应的双元音；若不存在对应双元音，则取前一个元音，第二个元音的时值通常会空置。 若双元音和短元音连缀，优先取对应的三元音；若不存在对应的三元音，那么短元音在前时，取双元音第一个元音，按照短元音连缀的规则进行缩合；若双元音在前，则单元音脱落。 如果是派生、变形所致，那么在正字法中，会写作缩合后的形式；如果是词间连缀，发音上遵从规则，但不影响拼写。

docs.title: 1.2.2 辅音连缀
docs.text: 图斯克语的辅音连缀，总体上而言遵从向前合并的原则，即同位置或同发音方式的辅音连缀时，倾向于向前合并。当然也存在各种情况的特例，并且也根据具体方言的不同而有所不同。

docs.title: 1.3 重音
docs.text: 重音通常在词干的第一音节上，若前缀为弱化类或单音节前缀，通常，重音仍在核心词根的第一音节。

docs.title: 1.4 历时音变
docs.text: 本节描述的是从拉埃拉德原始语到海峡图斯克语所发生的可总结的系统性历时音变，在实际的图斯克语和拉埃拉德原始语的比较中也发现存在各种个例，仅供研究者和读者参考。

docs.title: 1.4.1 元音
docs.text: 规则编号 规则形式 条件/环境 V 1 V → Ṽ / 元音邻近鼻辅音 V 2 Vː → VV → 短元音合并裂化 长元音裂化为双元音 V 3 V → [+前] / {t, d}  V → [+后] / {k, g} 前/后辅音环境 V 4 VV → Ṽ / [+鼻音]  VV → V / elsewhere 双元音邻近鼻音鼻化，否则保留 V 5 双元音后元音脱落 → 单元音 腭化辅音间环境

docs.title: 1.4.2 辅音
docs.text: 规则编号 规则形式 条件/环境 C 1 d → dz / σ[-重音] 非重读音节首 C 2 ts → tɕʰ / {i}  ts → tsʰ / {ɔ, u} C 3 送气辅音保留对立 所有环境 C 4 浊塞音去送气化 与清送气对立 C 5 ts → dz / VV 元音间位置 C 6 词尾鼻音脱落，元音鼻化 音节尾鼻音 V 1 → C 6 C 7 d → dʑ / {i, e}  d → {d, dz} / elsewhere 前元音触发颚化 C 8 z → ʑ / [-重音] ∪ # 非重读音节或词尾 C 9 词尾塞音脱落 所有塞音在词尾 C 10 词尾擦音/流音弱化 s, z, l, ɾ在词尾 C 11 声门擦音 h 脱落 词首 C 12 部分颤音 r 脱落 元音间 C 13 ʂ → {ʑ} / {i}ʂ → {s, z} / elsewhere

docs.title: 1.4.3 其他
docs.text: 规则编号 规则形式 条件/环境 O 1 l~ɾ → w / {o, u} ∪ # 邻近圆唇元音或词尾 O 2 词尾塞音弱化为鼻音 开元音节尾塞音 O 3 多音节词尾音节脱落，前音节补偿音长 尾音节脱落补偿前音节元音 O 4 颚化擦音保留对立 闭音节辅音擦化

docs.title: 2.1 动词
docs.text: 本节详述标准图斯克语动词的形态变化系统。图斯克语动词呈现出典型的高度黏着特征，其形态变化不仅是丰富构词的手段，更是构建句法框架的核心机制。动词形态的运作遵循严密的层级逻辑：越靠近词根的成分，越倾向于改变词汇的基本语义（派生）；越远离词根的成分，越倾向于通过时、体、气等范畴将动词锚定在具体的句法语境中（屈折）。

docs.title: 2.1.1 形态结构总览
docs.text:
图斯克语动词的形态结构可被视为一个以词根为核心的同心层级系统。其基本框架遵循 前缀 + 词根 + 后缀 的线性序列：

\[\mathbf{Prefixes} \text{ (主体/内涵)} + \mathbf{ROOT} \text{ (词根)} + \mathbf{Suffixes} \text{ (客体/外延)}\]

在这一框架下，各词缀占据固定的句法槽位，其完整生成序列如下：

\[\mathbf{[P_1 否定]} + \mathbf{[P_2 语态/减价]} + \mathbf{ROOT} + \mathbf{[S_1 增价]} + \mathbf{[S_2 态/方式]} + \mathbf{[S_3 气/时]}\]

各层级功能界定如下：

  1. 前缀区（内涵域）：

    • \(P_1\) 否定：位于最外层，对整个动词概念进行逻辑否定。

    • \(P_2\) 语态/减价：处理动作的主体关系及配价削减（如被动、反身），改变主语的角色性质。

  2. 词根（ROOT）：承载核心语义概念。

  3. 后缀区（外延域）：

    • \(S_1\) 增价（派生层）：最紧密的派生词缀，引入新的核心论元（如致使者、受益者），直接改变动词的配价结构。

    • \(S_2\) 态/方式（词汇层）：描述动作进行的内部方式或状态（如彻底、急速），修饰动作本身的性质。

    • \(S_3\) 气/时（屈折层）：最外层的句法标记，负责标示语气（虚拟）和时态（未来），完成动词与句子的语法对接。

docs.title: 2.1.2 配价系统
docs.text:
配价（Valency） 指动词所能支配的核心论元（主语、宾语等）数量。它是图斯克语动词分类的基石：

  • 零价动词：无逻辑论元，主要用于描述自然现象（如“下雨”）。

  • 一价动词（不及物）：仅支配一个施事论元（主语）。

  • 二价动词（及物）：支配施事（主语）与受事（宾语）。

  • 三价动词（双及物）：支配施事、受事及与事/受益者。

docs.title: 2.1.3 前缀区：语态与否定
docs.text:
前缀主要负责处理动作的“减法”操作（否定或减少论元）及主语角色的调整。

槽位 类别 词缀 名称 功能/语义 P1 否定 a 词汇否定 构建反义概念，直接否定动作或状态。 P2 语态 (减价) kwa 被动态 降级施事，提升受事为主语，配价-1。 zi 反身态 施事同时也是受事，动作回归自身，配价-1。 ma 交互态 多个施事相互作为受事，强调共同参与，配价-1。
  • 例句 A：交互态 (Reciprocal)

展示 \(P_2\) ma- 如何处理群体行为，将“模仿”变为“互相模仿”。

原文：kahya mapaubau

词间对照：海鸥 交互-模仿

中译：海鸥们在互相模仿。

解析：

  • 词根：paubau (模仿)。

  • 前缀：加上 ma- 后，配价减少，表示主语内部（海鸥群体）互为动作的施事和受事。

  • 例句 B：否定被动态 (Negated Passive)

展示 \(P_1\) a- 和 \(P_2\) kwa- 的叠加，用于描述某种状态的缺失。

原文：sywas akwadisyonau

词间对照：序列 否定-被动-引导

中译：该序列未被引导（处于无序/失控状态）。

解析：

  • 词根：disyonau (引导)。

  • Step 1：kwa-disyonau (被引导)。

  • Step 2：a-kwadisyonau (未被引导)。

  • 这种结构常用于描述抽象概念的状态。

docs.title: 2.1.4 后缀区：派生与屈折
docs.text:
后缀区严格遵循 \(S_1\) 增价 \(\to\) \(S_2\) 态 \(\to\) \(S_3\) 屈折 的顺序。这种排列反映了人类认知的自然顺序：先确定“谁参与动作（增价）”，再描述“动作怎么做（态）”，最后确定“动作何时/是否发生（时/气）”。

槽位 类别 词缀 名称 功能/语义 S1 增价 (派生) de 使动 引入致使者（使...做），配价+1。 rit 受益 引入受益者（为...做），配价+1。 sek 强迫 引入受迫者（逼...做），配价+1。 S2 态 (方式) morg 彻底态 强调动作完成的彻底性与完结性。 soi 急速态 强调动作发生的速度与紧迫感。 twa 撤销态 逆转已发生的动作（Undo）。 bor 起始态 聚焦于动作的起始阶段（Ingressive）。 mau 佯装态 描述动作的非真实性或模拟性质。 klo 误行态 表示动作执行错误或结果偏离预期。 lu 企图态 强调尝试与意图，不保证结果。 S3 气/时 (屈折) es 虚拟气 标记非现实情境（假设、条件）。通常先于时态后缀。 in 未来时 标记动作发生于说话时刻之后。
  • 例句 A：使动态 (Causative) - S1 层

展示 \(S_1\) de 如何改变词义，将“懂”变为“解释/阐明”。

原文：ia gamaide mboem

词间对照：我 懂-使动 他=NOM.ACC

中译 我使他明白/我给他讲解。

解析：

  • 词根：gamai (懂/理解)。

  • 派生：gamai-de 字面义为“使人懂”，在图斯克语中固化为“讲解”或“启蒙”的意思。

  • 例句 B：佯装态 (Pretend) - S2 层

展示 \(S_2\) mau 描述动作的虚假性。

原文：kizy sisicmau

词间对照：鸟类 发疯-佯装

中译：那鸟在装疯。

解析：

  • 词根：sisic (发疯)。

  • 修饰：sisic-mau (假装发疯)。这里后缀直接修饰了动作的性质，而不改变论元关系。

  • 例句 C：复合后缀链 (Complex Suffix Chain)

展示完整的 词根 \(\to\) 增价 \(\to\) 方式 \(\to\) 时态 链条。

原文：nemludesoin

词间对照：转-使动-急速-未来

中译：将快速旋转（某物）。

解析：

这是一个高信息密度的动词结构，构建过程如下：

结构层级 语素 含义 逻辑演变 ROOT (词根) nemlu 转 (Turn) 基础动作（自身旋转） + S1 (增价) -de 使动 (Make) nemlu-de (使旋转/Twist) 词义变为及物动词 + S2 (方式) -soi 急速 (Rapid) nemlu-de-soi (猛拧/Spin fast) 修饰动作方式 + S3 (时态) -in 未来 (Future) nemludesoiin (将要猛拧) 锚定时间

docs.title: 2.1.5.1 未来时 (Future Tense)
docs.text:
未来时通过黏着后缀 -in 标记，位于动词形态的最外层。

例句 3

原文：heimir ileyazin

词间对照：太阳=SG.NOM.DEF 发光=FUT

中译：太阳将发光

解析：ileyaz（发光）+ in（未来时）。

docs.title: 2.1.5.2 反复体 (Repetitive Aspect)
docs.text:
反复体是图斯克语中独特的形态手段，它不占据常规后缀槽位，而是通过词根重叠（Reduplication）实现的。它是动词内部的韵律构词手段，通常被视为发生在 \(S_1\) 之前的基础形变。

  • 规则：

    • 开音节动词：重复末音节 + 后缀 -n。

    • 闭音节动词：重复最后一个元音+辅音对（VC）。

例句 7

原文：okoir kegeg

词间对照：鸟=SG.NOM.DEF 吃~REP

中译：那鸟一直吃

解析：词根 keg（吃）为闭音节，重复韵尾 eg 形成 kegeg，表示动作持续。

当反复体与其他后缀共现时，重叠形式作为词干接受后续黏着：

例句 8

原文：okoir kegegin

词间对照：鸟=SG.NOM.DEF 吃~REP=FUT

中译：那鸟将会一直吃

解析：keg \(\to\) kegeg (反复词干) + in (未来时)。

docs.title: 2.1.5.3 虚拟语气 (Subjunctive Mood)
docs.text:
虚拟语气标记 -es 属于 \(S_3\) 屈折层，用于表达假设、条件或非实然状态。

  1. 独立使用：通常配合条件连词 selip（如果）。

    例句 10

    原文：selip barages

    词间对照：如果 下雨-SBJV

    中译：如果下雨

  2. 复合屈折：当虚拟语气与时态共现时，遵循 语气 (\(S_{3a}\)) + 时态 (\(S_{3b}\)) 的内部顺序，无需连词即可表达复杂的时空假设。

    例句 11

    原文：baragesin

    词间对照：下雨-SBJV-FUT

    中译：如果（将来）会下雨

    解析：barag (词根) + es (虚拟) + in (未来)。结构清晰地展示了“对未来发生的动作进行假设”。

docs.title: 2.1.5.4 例句：反身与误行 (Reflexive & Mistake)
docs.text:
展示前缀与后缀同时作用于词根。

原文：zinoinklo

词间对照：反身-阻-误行

中译：不小心绊倒自己（误阻了自己）。

解析：

  • Prefix: zi- (反身，动作回向主语)。

  • Root: noin (阻碍/阻挡)。

  • Suffix: -klo (误行，表示意外或失误)。

  • 语境：常用来形容笨拙的行为。

docs.title: 2.1.5.5 例句：反复体 (Repetitive)
docs.text:
展示开音节词根的重叠规则。

原文：ulih msyasyan

词间对照：仅仅 冥想~REP

中译：仅仅一直在冥想。

解析：

  • 词根：msya (冥想)，开音节结尾。

  • 变形：重复末音节 sya + n \(\to\) msyasyan。

  • 语义：强调动作的持续不断或单调重复。

docs.title: 2.1.6.1.1 -yaz 形成、呈现态
docs.text:
后缀 -yaz 是最常见的形容词动词化后缀之一。当它附加于形容词词根后，通常构建一个呈现态（Emissive）或状态（Stative）动词，意为“发出...的光/性质”或“呈现...的样子”。

构词公式：

\[\mathbf{Adj. Root} + \text{-yaz} \to \mathbf{Verb}_{\text{intr.}}\]

示例分析：

词汇演变：ileyaz

  • 词根 (Root): ile (adj. 光亮的 / bright)

  • 派生后缀 (Suffix): -yaz (呈现 / emit)

  • 结果: ile + yaz \(\to\) ileyaz (v. 发光 / to shine)

docs.title: 2.1.6.1.2 -bor 形成（起动态 Inchoative）
docs.text:
后缀 -bor 用于构建起动动词，表示主语自然地“进入某种状态”或“变得具有某种性质”。

注：该后缀与动词屈折后缀 S2（起始态）同源，在派生层面它强调状态的变化过程。

构词公式：

\[\mathbf{Adj. Root} + \text{-bor} \to \mathbf{Verb}_{\text{intr.}} \text{ (变得...)}\]

示例分析：

词汇演变：aucibor

  • 词根 (Root): auci (adj. 安静 / quiet)

  • 派生后缀: -bor (变得 / become)

  • 结果: auci + bor \(\to\) aucibor (v. 安静下来 / quieten down)

语境应用：

例句

原文：okoir auciborin

词间对照：鸟=NOM 安静-变得=FUT

中译：鸟儿将安静下来。

docs.title: 2.1.6.1.3 -de 形成（致使动态 Causative）
docs.text:
后缀 -de 用于构建及物动词，表示施事者将某种性质赋予受事者，即“使...具有某种性质”。

注：这是使动范畴在派生构词层面的直接体现，与动词增价后缀 S1（使动）共用同一语素，体现了图斯克语“词法-句法同构”的特点。

构词公式：

\[\mathbf{Adj. Root} + \text{-de} \to \mathbf{Verb}_{\text{tr.}} \text{ (使变成...)}\]

示例分析：

词汇演变：rolde

  • 词根 (Root): rol (adj. 深 / deep)

  • 派生后缀: -de (使... / make)

  • 结果: rol + de \(\to\) rolde (v. 加深，深化 / deepen)

语境应用：

例句

原文：ia roldein

词间对照：我 深-使=FUT

中译：导师将加深（这个坑/话题）。

docs.title: 2.1.6.1.4 -ic 形成（行事态 Simulative）
docs.text:
后缀 -ic 用于构建表示“表现得像...”或“以...的方式行事”的动词。它将形容词描述的静态特质转化为动态的行为风格。

构词公式：

\[\mathbf{Adj. Root} + \text{-ic} \to \mathbf{Verb}_{\text{intr.}} \text{ (表现得...)}\]

示例分析：

词汇演变：kwenic

  • 词根 (Root): kweni (adj./n. 愁，忧虑 / sorrowful)

  • 派生后缀: -ic (行事 / act like)

  • 结果: kweni + ic \(\to\) kwenic (v. 表现得忧愁，发愁 / mope, act sorrowful)

语境应用：

例句

原文：genir kwenicmau

词间对照：人=NOM 愁-行事-佯装

中译：那人在假装发愁。

解析：kweni (词根) \(\to\) kwenic (派生动词：表现忧愁) \(\to\) kwenic-mau (屈折：假装在表现忧愁)。

docs.title: 2.1.6.1.5 总结：形容词动词化后缀一览
docs.text:
后缀 名称 语义逻辑 关联章节 词例 (Adj → V) -yaz 呈现态 发出...光/性质 2.1.6.1.1 ile (亮) \(\to\) ileyaz (发光) -bor 起动态 变得... (进入状态) 2.1.4 (S2) auci (静) \(\to\) aucibor (安静下来) -de 致使态 使变成... (赋予性质) 2.1.4 (S1) rol (深) \(\to\) rolde (加深) -ic 行事态 表现得... (行为风格) 独有派生 kweni (愁) \(\to\) kwenic (发愁/表现忧愁) ### 2.2 名词 #### 2.2.1 主要词形变化

图斯克语名词格标记系统很规则，几乎没有例外。名词的主要变形如下表所示：

单数定指 单数非定指 复数定指 复数非定指 主格 -ir i, ... -wor nwi, ... 宾格 -im i, -em -wem nwi, -em 属格 -il i, -al -wil nwi, -al 与格 -ire i, -ce -wor nwi, -cei 方位/伴随格/工具格 -irt i, -it -wor (it) nwi, -it

图斯克语中，定指形式用后缀-ir, -wor 进行表示，相对的，非定指形式由冠词-i, -nwi 进行表示；复数非定指的冠词在海峡图斯克语和书面中是必须的，但是小部分方言里省去了复数非定指形式的冠词。

对单数名词，当定指形式和格标记同时出现时，部分格标记和定指形式发生了缩合，非定指形式由冠词标记，则不与格标记产生变化；对于复数名词，格标记会产生类似复数形变的变化。

docs.title: 2.2.1.1 格
docs.text:
在图斯克语的语法体系中，名词性成分的格位系统通常包含五个语法范畴，其中主格、宾格、与格、属格构成四大核心格位，另有一个附加格（涵盖方位、伴随或工具等语义）作为补充。以图斯克语为例：主格通过形态标记显性编码句法主语，即谈话的核心主题或动作的发起主体；宾格则特指及物动词的直接宾语，承担动作的承受者角色；与格对应间接宾语，标记动作的接受方或受益者；属格表达所属关系，构成名词间的从属关联。附加格作为多功能标记，依据语境动态呈现方位（处所）、伴随（协同对象）或工具（手段载体）等外围语义角色，通常伴随介词出现，由不同的介词具体承载信息。

在新派的海峡图斯克语中，名词性成分的格位系统有进一步简化的倾向，非定指形式的后缀在口语中，常常失去形变；附加格有并入宾格的倾向。但在本书中我们不对其做出讨论，一切以标准的海峡图斯克语为准。

接下来我们通过一些具体的例子对图斯克语的名词格系统进行说明。

在例句 1 “heimir deciap gu”中，“heimir”即名词 “heim”“日”的主格单数定指形式，在表中亦有呈现。

来看另一个例子：

例句 16

原句：kenir keg gupo i okoem

词间对照：狗=主格单数定指 吃 过去时完成体 单数非定指 鸟=宾格

中译：那狗吃掉了一只鸟

这句话的主语为 “kenir”，其中“=ir”作为主格单数定指标记，指明了狗是吃这个动作的发起人，并且说明了只存在一只特定的狗；“keg gupo”是过去时完成体标记，此处不再赘述；“i okoem”即鸟的宾格单数非定指形式，这里“i”是单数非定指标记，而“=em”这个黏着的后缀则标记了该名词的宾格地位，也即“鸟”在这句话中是动作的接受者。

由于图斯克语存在格标记，陈述句的语序实际上可以很灵活，例如：

例句 16

原句：i okoem kenir keg gupo

词间对照：单数非定指 鸟=宾格 狗=主格单数定指 吃 过去时完成体

中译：一只鸟（被）那狗吃掉了

在这句话中，语序实际上变成了“宾格-主格-动词”的结构，通过宾语的前置实现了对宾语的强调，在具体语境下可能是在强调被狗吃掉的东西是鸟。在中文我们或许可以将其翻译成被动句，但是在语义上并不能完全等同，尤其是考虑到中文中的被动句多数具有一种贬义。

docs.title: 2.2.1.2 定指
docs.text:
图斯克语的冠词系统通过形态手段对定指性和数范畴进行双重区分，形成四类显性标记。定指性单数采用后缀标记 -ir ，其实现形式直接附着于名词词干（如 naamar "家族" → naamar-ir "该家族"），而不定指单数则以前置独立冠词 i 构成（如 i naamar "一个家族"）。复数范畴的编码呈现不对称性：定指复数以后缀 -wor 标记（如 naamer-wor "这些家族"），不定指复数则依赖独立冠词 nwori（口语变体 nwi）前置（如 nwori naamer "多个家族"）。

该系统的形态实现受到词干音系结构的严格制约。当名词词干以元音结尾时，后缀 -ir 与-wor 需通过连音规则与词干元音衔接。独立冠词 nwori 在辅音丛语境下常发生音节缩减，其弱化形式 nwi 多见于非正式语域。

对复数定指形变而言，复数音变规则优先作用于词干，定指标记（-ir, -wor）随后依定指性选择，形态衔接受连音规则与语源音系特征约束。

数范畴 定指形式 不定指形式 示例（词干：namar "家族"） 单数 后缀-ir 冠词 i + 词干 naamarir "该家族"；i naamar "一个家族" 复数 后缀-wor 冠词 nwo (ri) + 词干 naamerwor "这些家族"；nwi naamer "多个家族"

注：后缀形态受词干尾音制约，冠词 nwori 在口语中可简化为 nwi，其形式选择受语域及节律规则调节。

docs.title: 2.2.2 语义类别
docs.text:
图斯克语名词可按含义分成五类。

第一类是抽象名词，表达情感、状态、概念等不具有可直接感知的实体的事物。

第二类是具体名词，表示可通过各种感官感知的、数量可分辨的实体。

第三类是物质名词，表示某些不可数的物质或材料，这一类名词在作形态变化时有特殊的处理方式：大多数的物质名词强制绑定复数形式，在涉及对该种类物质的描述时，通常加上黏着的后缀“=lis”，这在后文的名词派生中也有提到，但并不是所有能够通过“=lis”派生的名词都属于这一类，例如“wise”树，这一个名词就是一个具体名词，但它也可以通过“=lis”派生出名词“wiselis”意思是“这一种树”。

第四类是专有名词，是对特定地点、事物、个体、机构等的描述，通常不作变化，在图斯克语中，大量咕洛语的宗教性质借词也属于这一类。

第五类是时间名词，用于表示时间单位或时间段，这类名词相对封闭，很少有变化。

docs.title: 2.2.3 派生
docs.text: 名词派生主要通过后缀将动词、形容词或其他名词转化为具有特定实体意义的名词。

docs.title: 2.2.3.1 去动词名词
docs.text:
\[\text{名词} \leftarrow \text{动词} + \text{后缀}\] 后缀 功能标签 含义说明 示例 -ke 施事者 动作的执行者 baguke (杀手) -sar 专家/职业 职业性或习惯性的执行者 kegsar (美食家/老饕) -hel 受事 动作的直接承受对象 dibhel (愿景/被想之物) -laft 产物/结果 动作产生的残留物或结果 metwalaft (呕吐物) -jo 工具 实现动作的器具 gudujo (针/缝具) -bjo 容器 盛放动作对象的器皿 bagebjo (杯/壶) -mart 场所 动作发生的典型地点 haimemart (床/寝塌) -sait 抽象过程 动作的概念化状态 turhaisait (战争) -ik 单次事件 动作的一次具体发生 lebik (一次来访)

值得一提的是，有别于图斯克语简单陈述句中的一般词序，去动词派生模式如果涉及到动词的受事者时，受事者会在动词前出现

  • 句法模式 (SVO)：动词 + 宾语。用于描述具体的、有时态的事件。例如 Gago i japem（正在切一块肉）。

  • 构词模式 (OV)：宾语 + 动词。宾语前置，标志着宾语作为动词的附属，也有说法认为是亲代语言的残留，形成一个固有的概念。

此规则常用于构建职业身份（如 yuci-gudu-ke 缝衣人）、专用工具（如 Jap-gago-jo 切肉刀）以及固有活动类型（如 las-gago 理发）。

docs.title: 2.2.3.2 去形容词名词
docs.text: \[\text{名词} \leftarrow \text{形容词} + \text{后缀}\] 后缀 功能标签 含义说明 示例 -sait 性质 抽象的属性概念 ansait (善良/好) -net 度量 属性的量度/尺寸 lainet (大小/尺寸) -ke 拟人/物 具有该属性的人或物 norke (新人/新手)

docs.title: 2.2.3.3 名词内在转类
docs.text: \[\text{名词} \leftarrow \text{名词} + \text{后缀}\] 后缀 功能标签 含义说明 示例 -ino 指小 小的、幼年的、亲昵的 komino (小孩) -la 指大 巨大的、宏伟的 goiraula (大殿) -az 贬义 坏的、腐烂的、劣质的 japaz (腐肉) -lis 集合 作为一个类属的整体 wiselis (植被) -yasli 领域 某物主导的区域 wiseyasli (林地)

docs.title: 2.4.1.1 人称代词
docs.text:
尊卑层级 第一人称 第二人称 第三人称 特称 Sekui（朕） Kuisou（陛下） — 尊称 hirin （本人） 单复数同形 sorin（您） 单复数同形 mibok（怹） 单复数同形 北部：omsou （阁下） 南部：lakome （大人） 复数：lakomewor 基础/中性 ia （我） iaki sou （你） souki mbo（他） mboki 自指/谦称 通用：wan （在下） 男性：wak （鄙） /sewank （此身） — 女性：haam（妾）/sewam （此身） —

图斯克语人称代词系统通过多层次的形态分化和词汇选择，构建起反映社会等级制度的称谓体系。其基础人称系统包含 ia（我）、sou（你）、mbo（他/她/它）三个中性代词，复数形式通过后缀 -ki 构成（如 iaki“我们”、souki“你们”、mboki“他们”）。这一底层系统服务于日常平等交际，而更复杂的尊卑层级则通过特定称谓实现。

同时，图斯克语的人称代词存在尊称上的分化：

君主自称 Sekui（朕），用以和一般人自称做区分；臣民对君主使用 Kuisou（陛下），两者都凸显了皇权身份的尊贵。

社会尊称则呈现地域分化：第一人称尊称 hirin（本人/吾）单复数同形，用于上位者自称；第二人称北部使用 omsou（阁下），南部使用 lakome（大人），后者复数通过添加定指后缀 -wor 构成 lakomewor。第三人称尊称 mibok 源于复数词干 mboki 的语音演变，单复数通用，常用于称呼神祇，在图斯克-黑蔑特一带仅用于称呼神祇。

自指谦称系统以通用形式 wan（在下）为核心，通过形态变化实现性别分化：男性使用辅音强化形式 wak 或带前缀的 sewank，女性则为 haam 或 sewam。整个体系通过复数后缀 -ti 、定指复数 -wor 及语音演变（如辅音强化、元音交替），将社会层级编码于语言形式之中，映射出严格的尊卑秩序与地域文化差异

docs.title: 2.4.1.2 指示代词
docs.text:
图斯克语的基础指示代词是二分的，se 表示近指的单数形式，sa 表示远指，直接继承自原始语。

在句中作名词时，遵守名词的语法变形规则。若用作名词的修饰词，则由 sei，sawi 分别表示近指，远指的复数形式，作为定指后缀的替代。

远指代词，在复杂句中也作为从句结束的标志存在。

docs.title: 2.4.1.3 不定代词
docs.text:
图斯克语主要的不定代词如下表所示

拼写 释义 soazi 全部 soa 很多 soj 一些 hau 很少 asea 其他 soali 更多

不定代词虽然作为名词性成分，但是也可以被用于修饰名词，来限定名词。

docs.title: 2.4.2 介词
docs.text:
图斯克语的介词，可以作为独立成分使用，但是一般出现在附加格名词前作为黏着前缀。

拼写 释义 ow 在……上 eh 在……下 siuy 在……里面 yau 在……外面 we 从…… gri 沿着…… wi 在……附近

不同的动词可能与特定介词有着固定搭配。

docs.title: 2.4.3 数词
docs.text:
图斯克语的数词系统为十进制：下表是一到十以内的基数词的说法

拼写 释义 ita 一 sod 二 mib 三 badaa 四 asu 五 mo 六 ak 七 buzen 八 dziu 九 gi 十 gita 十一 giso 十二 gimb 十三 gibad 十四 giasu 十五 gimo 十六 giak 十七 gibuz 十八 gidziu 十九 sodgi 二十 sodgita 二十一 kaha 百 numrea 千

百以上的数词前，会加 i- 来表达仅有一的情况，例如：

一百 ikaha

一千一百二十一 inumrea ikahasodgita

序数词直接在基数词后面加 -k 实现。

docs.title: 2.4.4.1 并列连词
docs.text:
名词性成份间可用 ag 连接，来表达并列关系，例如：

ita ku ag ita mea 一个男人和一个女人。

当存在多个并列成份时，书面中应用 ag 依次连接，例如：

ita ku ag ita mea ag ita normea 一个男人和一个女人和一个女孩。但在口语中，最后一个 ag 之前的 ag 常常被省略。

docs.title: 2.4.5 叹词
docs.text:
图斯克语的叹词较为简单：

he 表示犹豫、厌恶等，具体依声调而定。

ala 表示惊讶、也表示惊喜。

ma 表示否定，也表示就这样吧，也依声调而定。

docs.title: 3.1 基本语序与话题结构
docs.text:
图斯克语在句法类型学上属于典型的话题优先语言。虽然格标记系统允许 SVO 或 SOV 等多种底层语序共存，但在实际的语言运用中，句子的第一个成分总是被预设为话题，即谈话的中心对象或背景框架，而后续成分则是对该话题的陈述。

这种“话题优先”配合“定语后置”，使得图斯克语的句子往往以一个最为核心的名词（裸露或带定指）开场，随后紧跟其修饰语，最后是谓语陈述。

docs.title: 3.1.1 话题前置规则
docs.text:
无论是主语、宾语，还是时间、地点状语，只要它是说话人想要强调的“已知信息”，就会被移至句首。

  • 主语作为话题：

    Kenir lai, japem keg gu.

    词间对照： 狗=定指 大，肉=宾格 吃 过去时。

    中译： （那只）大狗吃了肉。

  • 宾语作为话题： 当宾语前置时，其宾格标记保留，构成类似汉语“把字句”或“话题化”的结构。

    Japem, kenir lai keg gu.

    词间对照： 肉=宾格，狗=定指 大 吃 过去时。

    中译： （至于）那块肉，大狗吃了它。

docs.title: 3.2.1 中心语前置与定语后置
docs.text:
在标准海峡图斯克语中，名词短语的内部结构遵循严格的中心语前置原则。这意味着核心名词总是位于短语的最前端，而所有的修饰性成分——无论是形容词、领属者还是关系从句——都跟随在核心名词之后。

这一特征使图斯克语在听感上呈现出一种“层层递进”的逻辑：先抛出核心概念，随后再对其性状、归属进行限定。

译者注：这与现代汉语或英语的习惯（形容词在名词前）截然不同，而更接近于越南语或希伯来语的逻辑。

docs.title: 3.2.2 形容词修饰
docs.text:
形容词直接置于被修饰的名词之后。 值得注意的是，名词的定指后缀（-ir/-wor）以及格标记（-im/-il 等）依然附着在核心名词上，而非移动到整个短语的末尾。

例句 17

原句：kenir lai

词间对照：狗=单数主格定指 大

中译：那只大狗

例句 18

原句：baaja dziapk

词间对照：海 碧蓝的

中译：碧蓝的大海

docs.title: 3.2.3 从属
docs.text:
领属结构同样遵循后置原则，即 [被拥有者] + [拥有者-属格]。 当存在多层领属关系时，这种结构会形成一种链式延伸。

例句 19

原句：syecir tesukuil

词间对照：剑=单数主格定指 铁苏库=单数属格定指

中译：铁苏库的剑

例句 20

原句：syecir pail ial

词间对照：朋友=单数主格定指 父亲=单数属格非定指 第一人称属格

中译：我父亲的剑

docs.title: 3.3 动词短语
docs.text: 图斯克语的动词短语，其内部配列和名词短语趋同，遵从中心语前置，状语后置的顺序。

docs.title: 3.3.1 句法时
docs.text:
图斯克语的时态系统呈现不对称的三分结构，过去时和现在时分别存在独立的语法标记词，而未来时则通过黏着的语法后缀表达。考虑到拉埃拉德原始语实际上存在一个对称的三分时态系统，可能在演化的过程中图斯克语先是丢掉了其中的未来时，变成一个二分的语法结构。从一个浪漫的角度推测，很可能是大灾变时代的到来导致人们对于未来失去了希望，更倾向于总结过去的经验以及安排当下的事情。

总而言之在简单句这一章节，我们主要讨论之前在构词中没有讨论的句法时，也即图斯克语时态系统中除了 -in 以外的两个，以助词为载体的句法层面上的时。这两个时的形态相对独立，在句子中的位置也相对灵活。

docs.title: 3.3.1.1 过去时
docs.text:
图斯克语的过去时使用独立的标记词"gu"实现，用来表示事件发生在说话人讲述时间的过去，是一种相对时态。标记词一般紧跟在动词之后。

例句 1

原文：heimir deciap gu.

词间对照翻译：太阳=单数主格定指 升起 过去时.

中译：（那）太阳升起了

graph TD
    S --> NP
    S --> VP
    NP --> N
    NP --> DET_Case
    N[heim]
    DET_Case[ir]
    VP --> V
    VP --> Tns
    V[deciap]
    Tns[gu]

例句 1 是一个很简单的例子，在这里只有一个名词性成分，即是话题焦点，也是所谓的主语成分。“heim”是图斯克语中对太阳这一概念的一种表述方式，但是这里用到的形式是“heimir”，是名词“heim”的单数定指形式，在词间对照翻译中也看到，“=ir” 即单数主格定指后缀。也就是说，“heimir”表达的概念近似于“（那）太阳”，在现代汉语的表达里没有严格对应的，或许可以类比于现代英语中的“The sun”。“deciap”是图斯克语表示“升起”这一含义的一种方式，应该是源自原始语，“de=”可以表达使动的含义，“deciap”可以被理解为“使高”。”gu“即图斯克语的独立过去时标记词，一般紧跟在所修饰的动词之后。

docs.title: 3.3.1.2 现在时
docs.text:
当不添加标记时，也就是图斯克语动词通常状态下的词典形，一般视作动词的现在时，也称一般时，多用于描述普遍存在的现象和持续的、未完的动作。可以通过独立标记词“ho” 强调事件与谈话的同时性，也即进行体，这在后文对体的叙述中有所提及。

例句 2

原句：betumartirt nwi aguisib wise ar

词间对照翻译：寒带地区=单数从格定指 复数非定指冠词 针叶的 树 存在

中译：在寒带有针叶林

graph TD
    S --> PP
    S --> VP

    %% PP 部分
    PP --> P
    P[Ø]
    PP --> NP1
    NP1 --> DET1
    DET1[DET+CASE: irt]
    NP1 --> N1
    N1[betumart]

    %% VP 部分
    VP --> V
    V[ar]
    VP --> NP2
    NP2 --> DET2
    DET2[PL-IND: nwi]
    NP2 --> Nbar
    Nbar --> Adj
    Adj[aguisib]
    Nbar --> N2
    N2[wise]

这句话包含了不止一个语法成分，我们先集中看本小节所要阐述的现在时（一般时），也就是“ar”这个动词在这里所处的形式。“ar”没有任何变形，意味着它既不表达过去，也不表达未来，而是表达一个宽泛的时间概念，陈述了这样一个事实，即“在寒带有针叶林，过去有，未来可能还有”。如果我们要强调当下在寒带有针叶林，可以用后文提及的进行体来表达。

docs.title: 3.3.2.1 完成体
docs.text:
图斯克语通过添加标记词"po"表示动作彻底完成且结果存续。若存在时态标记词，则和时态标记词合并作为一个标记。

例句 3.3.2.1.1

原句：heimir ebmoi po

词间对照翻译：太阳=主格单数定指 落下 完成体

中译：太阳落下了

例句 3.3.2.1.1 是 heimir emboi po，通过完成体标记表示“太阳已经落下”，重点在强调太阳落下这一动作已经完成，并且现在太阳仍处于落下的状态。

例句 3.3.2.1.2

原句：heimir ebmoi gu po

词间对照翻译：太阳=主格单数定指 落下 过去时完成体

中译：太阳（在过去）落下了

例句 3.3.2.1.2heimir ebmoi gu po，通过过去时-完成体的缩合词，表达了“太阳在过去就已经落下”的意思，强调动作完成的同时，也提及了主题相对于谈话时的时间。

例句 3.3.2.1.3

原句：heimir ebmoin po

词间对照：太阳=主格单数定指 落下=未来时 完成体

中译：太阳（还会）落下

例句 3.3.2.1.3 不是一个常见的说法，未来时和完成体的连用，可以有两种解读：“太阳（总是）还会落下”，“太阳在未来的时间已经落下”。这个时态通常用于强调某种存续性，理论上来说可以存在这样的搭配，但在实际运用中并不常见。

docs.title: 3.3.2.2 进行体
docs.text:
图斯克语的有 ho 作为独立标记词表示动作的持续性，未完成状态。

例句 7

原句：heimir ileyaz ho

词间对照： 太阳=主格单数定指 发光 进行体

中译：太阳正在发光

heimir 表示“太阳”，而 ileyaz ho 通过进行体标志 ho 强调了太阳 正在 发光，重点是该动作与对话时刻的共时性。

docs.title: 3.3.3 句法情态
docs.text: 除了前文所述的图斯克语的情态词缀以外，图斯克语海存在情态动词，位于动词派生结构之外，负责表达说话人的主观态度（意愿、判断）或客观条件（能力、可能性）。情态动词作为独特的一类动词，在后接从句或构词时不需要额外的助词 sam。

docs.title: 3.3.3.1 祈使
docs.text:
祈使语气通过情态动词 pa 或 pasingo 表达对他人的命令或者祈使，一般只针对第二人称。 pa 近似于命令，pasingo 则更近似于 请 的礼貌表述，

以下是两个例子

例句 2.1

原句: sou pa keg

词间对照：第二人称单数代词 命令式 吃

中译：你吃吧！

例句 2.2

原句：sorin pasingo keg

词间对照：第二人称单数尊称形代词 祈使式 吃

中译：您请吃

“sorin”在图斯克语中是一个第二人称的尊称形式，可以理解为“您”。

docs.title: 3.3.3.2 必然
docs.text:
图斯克语用 "ying" 独立结构，作为必然情态的标记。

例句 14

原句：ying baragin

词间对照翻译：必然态 下雨=未来时

中译：将来一定下雨

这句话很简单，用“ying”修饰了 “baragin”表达对未来的一种强预测，表达出中文“一定”、“必然”之类的概念。

docs.title: 3.3.3.3 意愿
docs.text:
图斯克语的意愿情态，通过独立的"tyaa"（想），表达主语想要做某事，有做某事的意愿。

例句 13

原句：ia tyaa keg

词间对照：第一人称单数代词 意愿态 吃

中译：我想吃

用“tyaa”表达意愿态，同时“tyaa”后一般跟动词词典形，在这里也就是无任何其他语法标记的“keg”。

docs.title: 3.3.3.4 能力
docs.text:
图斯克语通过情态动词 "ning"（能），表达主语能够做某事，有能力做某事，通常紧接在主语后。

例句 14

ia ning bagues gu mboem

词间对照：第一人称单数代词 能力态 杀=虚拟体 过去时 第三人称宾格

中译：我本来能杀他

docs.title: 3.4 系词句 (判断句)
docs.text: 图斯克语中不存在相当于现代汉语“是”或英语“be”的系动词。系词句的构建依赖于名词性成分或形容词性成分的直接并列，即所谓的零系词结构。

docs.title: 3.4.1 零系词结构
docs.text:
在一般现在时中，句子由 [话题] 和 [说明语] 直接构成。 由于图斯克语采用定语后置，为了区分“名词+修饰语”（名词短语）和“主语+形容词谓语”（句子），口语中依赖明显的停顿，书面语中则常以逗号或助词 ne 标示话题的结束。

  • 形容词谓语：停顿

    例句 21

    原句：Kenir, lai (ne)

    词间对照：狗=定指 （停顿） 大 （句末助词）

    中译：那只狗是大的。

    对比： Kenir lai （那只大狗——名词短语，非完整句子）

  • 名词谓语：通过格标记谓语

    例句 22

    原句：Ia Jaujyaem.

    词间对照：第一人称 卫士宾格

    中译：我是卫士。

docs.title: 3.4.2 时态变化
docs.text:
当需要表达过去或未来的判断时，时态标记词直接附着在谓语（名词或形容词）之后。

  • 过去时： 使用独立标记词 gu。

    Mbo Jaujyaem gu. —— 他曾是卫士。

  • 未来时： 形容词或名词通常先接动词化后缀（如 -yaz），再接未来时后缀 -in；但在口语中，也常直接将 -in 附着于词尾。

    Heimir ileyazin. —— 太阳会变亮/太阳将会是亮的。

docs.title: 3.4.3 否定判断
docs.text:
使用否定副词 mage 置于谓语说明语之前。

例句 23

原句：Mbo mage Jaujyaem.

词间对照：第三人称 不 卫士=宾格标记

中译：他不是卫士。

docs.title: 3.5 比较句
docs.text: 由于图斯克语采用定语后置的语序，比较结构也遵循“中心语+修饰成分”的逻辑。比较级通常通过介词前缀 we-（源自/从……）引出的介词短语来实现，该短语修饰形容词谓语。

docs.title: 3.5.1 比较级
docs.text:
基本语序为：[话题 A] + [形容词] + [we-比较对象 B]。 这在字面意义上理解为：“A 是大的，相对于（从） B”。

例句 24

原句：Mbo lai we-iat.

词间对照：第三人称单数 大 从-我=方位格

中译：他比我大。

docs.title: 3.5.2 最高级
docs.text:
最高级通过助词 zi 实现

例句 25

原句：Sakha auqy zi.

词间对照：那里 安静 最高级修饰

中译：那里是最安静的。

docs.title: 4.1 名词性从句
docs.text: 名词性从句在主句中充当主语、宾语、表语或同位语。图斯克语根据从句是否具备独立的时态与陈述功能，严格区分有限从句与无限从句，并分别采用不同的语序。

docs.title: 4.1.1 宾语从句
docs.text:
作及物动词的直接宾语。

  1. 有限宾语从句用于表达独立的陈述事实，时态相对于主句独立。必须由宾语从句引导词 maem 引导，从句保持 SVO (主-谓-宾) 语序。

例句 29

原句：ia teis maem [mbo bagu gu migem].

词间对照：我 知 引导词 [他 杀 过去时 龙=宾格]

中译：我知道[他杀了龙]。

  1. 无限宾语从句用于控制动词（如“尝试”、“想要”、“开始”），强调动作与主句的紧密结合。此类从句无引导词且强制采用 OV (宾-谓) 语序，将动词置于最后以示从属。

例句 30

原句：Ia tauh [migem bagu].

词间对照：我 尝试 [龙=宾格 杀]

中译：我尝试[杀龙]。

docs.title: 4.1.2 主语从句
docs.text:
作全句的语法主语，通常置于句首，由引导词 mair 引导。

例句 31

原句：Mair [sou leb], an.

词间对照：引导词 [你 来]，好

中译：[你来了]这件事，很好。

docs.title: 4.2 形容词性从句 (关系从句)
docs.text: 受名词修饰语后置规则的影响，图斯克语的关系从句位于其修饰的先行词之后。关系从句采用缺空 (Gap) 策略，即从句中与先行词指代相同的成分被省略。为了标示修饰范围的结束，通常在从句末尾使用指示代词 sa（那）进行封口。

docs.title: 4.2.1 主语缺空
docs.text:
当先行词在从句中充当主语时。

例句 32

原句：Komeir [bagu gu migem sa] leb gu.

词间对照：人=定指 [杀 过去时 龙=宾格 那(封口)] 来 过去时

中译：[杀了龙的]那个人来了。

docs.title: 4.2.2 宾语缺空
docs.text:
当先行词在从句中充当宾语时，封口的助词采用分词 sam 。

例句 33

原句：Migir [komeir bagu gu sam] ud gu.

词间对照：虫=定指 [人=定指 杀 过去时 那(封口)] 死 过去时

中译：[那人杀掉的]那条虫死了。

docs.title: 4.3 副词性从句
docs.text: 副词性从句在复合句中充当状语，修饰主句的动词、形容词或整个命题。此类从句通常由连词引导，位置较为灵活，但倾向于前置于主句以设定背景。

docs.title: 4.3.1 时间状语从句
docs.text:
由连词 alsim (当...时) 或介词前缀 eh- (在...下/后) 引导。

例句 36

原句：alsim heimir ebmoi, ia ate.

词间对照：当 太阳 落下，我 睡

中译：当太阳落下时，我睡觉。

例句 37

原句：eh-turhaisaitirt, mbo leb gu.

词间对照：在-尾部(之后) 战争，他 来 过去时

中译：战争结束后，他来了。

docs.title: 4.3.2 地点状语从句
docs.text:
由连词 mart (在...的地方) 引导。此连词源自疑问词 ma (哪里) 与方位格 -irt 的缩合。

例句 38

原句：ia gre mart i wiselis ar.

词间对照：我 住 哪里 不定冠词 树 存在

中译：我住在有树的地方。

docs.title: 4.3.3 原因状语从句
docs.text:
由连词 garu (因) 引导。

例句 39

原句：garu mbo ud gu, ia dih gu.

词间对照：因 他 死 过去时，我 哭 过去时

中译：因为他死了，所以我哭了。

docs.title: 4.3.4 结果状语从句
docs.text:
采用 形容词 + soa (如此) ... olem (以至于) 的结构。或有 olem 单独存在作连词表达 结果 的意思。

例句 40

原句：wetir lai soa, olem wise syowo gu.

词间对照：风=定指 大 如此，[引导] 树 倒 过去时

中译：风大到树都倒了。

docs.title: 4.3.5 目的状语从句
docs.text:
由连词 tyaam (为了) 引导。因目的通常表示未然的、概念性的动作，强制使用 OV (宾-谓) 语序的无限从句结构。

例句 41

原句：tyaam [migem bagu], ia kwei gu syecim.

词间对照：为了 [龙=宾格 杀]，我 买 过去时 剑=宾格

中译：为了杀龙，我买了剑。

docs.title: 4.3.6 条件状语从句
docs.text:
由连词 selib (若/如果) 引导。若表达“除非”，则使用 selib mage (若不)。

例句 42

原句：selib baragin, ia mage jepo.

词间对照：若 下雨-未来，我 不 走

中译：如果下雨，我就不走。

docs.title: 4.3.7 让步状语从句
docs.text:
由连词 kamave (虽/即使) 引导。

例句 43

原句：kamave mbo ning, mbo mage deud.

词间对照：虽 他 能，他 不 弄死

中译：虽然他能（杀），但他没杀。

docs.title: 4.3.8 方式状语从句
docs.text:
由连词 kemse (像...若 / 好像) 引导。

例句 44

原句：Mbo keg, kemse mbo ud ho.

词间对照：他 吃，好像 他 死(饿死) 进行体

中译：他吃得像是快饿死了一样。

docs.title: 4.3.9 比较状语从句
docs.text:
图斯克语的比较通常通过介词 we- (从/比) 实现短语比较；若涉及句子比较，使用结构 kem (像)。

例句 45

原句：Mbo lai we-ia=rt.

词间对照：他 大 从-我=方位格

中译：他比我大。

例句 46

原句：Mbo bagu kem ia (bagu).

词间对照：他 杀 像 我 (杀)

中译：他像我一样杀戮。

docs.title: 4.3.10 转折状语从句
docs.text:
例句 47

原句：Mbo bagu ia=em, rage ia mage ud.

词间对照：他 杀 我=宾格 但 我 没 死

中译：他杀我但我没死

docs.title: 🌏 About Laaerad
docs.text:
Welcome to the fictional world of Laaerad. This is a world constructed based on the logic of real-world history, focusing on the evolution of civilizations, the flow and change of languages, and the rise and fall of dynasties.

Here, you can witness:

  • How the Trusk Empire grew from tribes into a colossal entity ruling the Northern Continent.

  • How Laprio and the Glömezher Tribes shaped vastly different cultures.

  • How the ancient Proto-Laaerad Language diverged, eventually forming the modern Truskean and Glömezher languages.

docs.title: 🧭 Explore
docs.text: If this is your first time here, it is recommended to start your journey in the following order:

docs.title: 🏛️ Trusk Empire
docs.text:
The most complete empire in the current setting.

The empire's history is written by these three major dynasties. Click to learn more:

Heimet Dynasty Tyesuku Dynasty Moswarike Dynasty Keywords: Architecture, Prosperity, Matashosa Gate Keywords: Conquest, Legal Code, Dezhohai Keywords: Reform, Civil War, Tuluhai Ke The Embryo and Origin of the Empire The Empire's Founder, an Era of Iron and Blood A Turbulent Period of Transition and Reshaping

docs.title: 🗺️ Geography and Culture
docs.text:
Overview of world geography and astronomy: Basic Setting of Laaerad.

Heimet Region: The power center of the Trusk Empire.

  • Hashet City: The empire's capital.

  • Matashosa Gate Road: The artery connecting the east and west of the empire.

docs.title: 📜 Immersive Reading
docs.text:
Don't want to read dry settings? Try observing the world through the eyes of characters from the books:

  • 📖 Wattsukasem Travelogue: Follow the author's footsteps to explore the secrets of Wattsukasem.

  • 🗣️ Glömezher Legends: Listen to the ancient myths of the nomadic tribes.

docs.title: 📅 Latest Updates
docs.text:
• Heimet Dynasty: Added details about late-period court coups.

  • Study of Common Names in the Trusk Region: Some common names in the Trusk region, including some names from Laprio and Glömezher languages.

  • Trusk Chronology: Added some details.

Built in 2025 | Laaerad Wiki Project All content currently included on this site is the personal creation of Phychias Lok (骆以川). © This Website is constructed by Phychias Lok, Phychiaslok@gmail.com.

docs.title: Main Figures
docs.text: • Tuluhai Ke: Known as the tenth Great Chieftain of the Guluo tribes, who later unified the entire Guluo region and founded the Moswarike Dynasty.

docs.title: Tuluhaike
docs.text: Tuluhaike was the tenth great chieftain of the [[Moswarike Dynasty]]. He consolidated and unified the Guluo Tribes, establishing a powerful and united army. He implemented a feudal system, safeguarded the rights of slaves and established a slave-soldier system, while secretly pledging allegiance to the Heimet central government to lull them into complacency. He established the intelligence agency "Molofa Organization" and an army independent of the Council of Elders to strengthen his rule. In the war against Basib, he achieved victory with the help of Spirit Speakers, entered Heimet, and conquered most of the regions south of the Truskean Basin. His name "Tuluhaike" means "combative" in Truskean, a nickname he earned for his fierce fighting on the battlefield.

docs.title: TyesukuTyesuku
docs.text: Tyesuku (Truskean: tyesuku) is a pivotal concept from the early imperial period of Laaerad. It refers both to the prominent family that ruled western Trusk—the Tyesuku family—and to the geographical name of their hereditary domain and the later-established Tyesuku Minor Kingdom.

docs.title: Etymology and Meaning
docs.text:
Linguists trace the term Tyesuku back to Proto-Laaeradian.

  • Root Composition: Derived from tyesu (warm, hot) and ku (stone).
  • Original Meaning: "Warm stone" or "hot stone." Scholars widely agree that this term initially served as a vivid description of the prevalent blacksmithing profession in the area—symbolizing the process of repeatedly heating and forging iron ore in a furnace.
  • Semantic Evolution: As a local family, originally akin to a blacksmiths' guild, gradually gained political power, the term evolved from a description of a profession/local product into a family surname and subsequently a place name.

docs.title: The Tyesuku Family
docs.text: The Tyesuku family is a top-tier aristocratic lineage that runs throughout Trusk history. Its family history is a microcosm of the power transitions within the Trusk Empire.

docs.title: Early Period: Latency and Rise
docs.text: Before the emergence of Tyesuku Webo, historical inscriptions contain only sporadic and marginal records of the family, mostly depicting them as minor local officials reporting to the capital. It wasn't until Tyesuku Webo became the family head that the family formally stepped onto the historical stage. During the period of the Second Heimet Dynasty, Webo utilized the internal turmoil of the dynasty to gradually accumulate political capital.

docs.title: The Tyesuku Dynasty
docs.text:
The family's first peak of power began with Dezhuohei I.

The third emperor of the Second Heimet Dynasty (the last king, Ansaite) had no direct male heirs. As the emperor's nephew (some sources say son-in-law and chancellor), Dezhuohei I pacified the realm in the chaotic period following a plague and ultimately took over the government, initiating the First Tyesuku Dynasty.

docs.title: Mid-Period: Latency During the Botessizhuahu Period
docs.text: The First Dynasty did not last long, and the empire subsequently fell under the rule of the Botessizhuahu Dynasty. Although the direct descendants of Dezhuohei I were integrated into the Botessizhuahu imperial family through marriage, the collateral branch of the Tyesuku family—the line of Webo II—did not die out. They were enfeoffed back to their ancestral land, Tyesuku City, where they bided their time and accumulated strength.

docs.title: The Second Tyesuku Dynasty
docs.text: Years later, Dezhuohei III, as the family leader, led the uprising that overthrew the Botessizhuahu Dynasty. He successfully ascended the imperial throne once again, establishing the Second Tyesuku Dynasty. He established the Trusk Empire's first truly centralized rule and is regarded by later generations as the empire's true imperial ruler.

docs.title: The Tyesuku Region (Tyesuku Minor Kingdom)
docs.text: The Tyesuku Region, administratively known as the Tyesuku Minor Kingdom.

docs.title: Historical Development
docs.text:
• Origin: Initially referred only to Tyesuku City at the end of the Kelusuofa River in the southern part of the Trusk Basin and its surroundings. This was the earliest fiefdom granted to the Tyesuku family during the Second Heimet Dynasty.
  • Expansion: Through Dezhuohei I's military campaigns and the later establishment of the Second Tyesuku Dynasty, the region was consolidated into a major kingdom, hereditarily governed by a prince of the Tyesuku family (the King of Tyesuku).

docs.title: Geographical Territory
docs.text:
The kingdom is located in the western part of the empire and primarily consists of the following regions:

  • Tyesuku Mountains: The natural barrier to the west.
  • Genara Highlands: A strategic highland connecting to the south.
  • Tyesuku Royal Domain: The core ruling area.
  • Andon Lands: An important agricultural and population area.
  • Pernan Lands: Located in the upper reaches of the Pernan River.

docs.title: Political System
docs.text: Unlike the commandery-county system in the empire's core regions, the Tyesuku Kingdom adopted a feudal system. Lands were primarily enfeoffed to generals who had performed outstanding military service in the unification wars and to the old retainers of the Tyesuku family (such as the collateral branches of the Webo line), forming a military aristocracy that guarded the empire's western flank.

docs.title: Related Entries
docs.text: Category Entry Notes Geography Trusk Region A core concept of the empire. Heimet Another royal domain of the empire, located northeast of Tyesuku. Figures Dezhuohei I Founder of the First Dynasty. Dynasties Tyesuku Dynasty The imperial dynasty established by the family.

docs.title: Decoheimdecoheim I
docs.text:
Wiki

Family Name Tyesuku Given Name Decoheim Year of Birth Heimet Second Dynasty, Oleman Year 13 Major Achievements Laid the groundwork for the various institutions of the Empire today;

Decoheim I, also known as Tyesuku Decoheim I (hereinafter referred to as Decoheim), is an important figure in the history of the Trusk Empire that cannot be ignored. During his reign, he laid the groundwork for the various institutions of the Empire today; it was also during this period that a cross-ethnic consciousness of imperial citizenship began to be established. Although his rule was full of turmoil and controversy, it nevertheless laid an indelible foundation for the long-lasting Tyesuku dynasty that followed. The emperors of the Tyesuku dynasty often paid homage to Decoheim in the imperial ancestral temple.

docs.title: Biography
docs.text: Tyesuku Decoheim, son of Weber Decoheim I, was nineteen years old when he was enfeoffed as the King of Tyesuku by Heimet Aset III. At the age of thirty, after Aset died without an heir, he usurped the throne.

docs.title: An Unusual Childhood
docs.text:
Decoheim was born on a crisp, clear autumn day. That day, two suns hung in the sky. Even in the imperial capital [[Hashite City]], where people of broad knowledge gathered, people did not consider two suns in the sky to be a common occurrence. The two suns did not make the city hotter, but the dogs in the city barked incessantly at the sky. So Decoheim's father named him Decoheim. Decoheim means "split sun," the act of making the sun become two. This name is recorded in the classics; presumably, someone long ago was also born on such a day with two suns in the sky.

Although two suns hung high in the sky on the day Decoheim was born, when he was born, he didn't seem any different from other children. However, by the age of three, he still couldn't speak, and most of the time he just stared blankly ahead, worrying all the adults of the Tyesuku family. In other aspects, he seemed normal and could apparently understand what the adults were saying. They searched all over Heimet for doctors, but most doctors could only shake their heads and sigh; few proposed unconventional methods, and Decoheim's mother, Visera, felt they were too risky. The closest to success was actually a monk from the nearby Tanka of the Zanghai Sect. That day, after practicing Zanghai Gong with him for a few moments, Decoheim uttered a few sporadic phonemes. However, Visera couldn't bear to send her only son to the Tanka to practice with those strange-looking monks and Jiaohuzayi. Decoheim's father, Weber, the Duke of Tyesuku, couldn't be bothered with that. Decoheim was his only son at the time, and he would never give up any hope of making his son normal.

From then on, for a period each lunar month, Decoheim, accompanied by servants or his mother, would go to the temple. Sometimes he went to listen to the monks tell myths and watch them perform rituals; sometimes he went to sit in a row with the equally taciturn Jiaohuzayi beside the temple's pointed little stone pagoda, quietly meditating. After about a year or two, Visera grew increasingly anxious. On one hand, Decoheim still showed little sign of improvement; on the other hand, when Decoheim was four, Weber's concubine gave birth to a son for Weber. This younger brother could speak by the age of one, appearing clever and quick-witted. Although Visera was the principal wife and was pregnant again at the time, she still couldn't help but worry that the next child might not be a boy, or that she might give birth to another little mute. Decoheim gradually grew accustomed to his regular visits to the temple. He seemed to get along fairly well with those Jiaohuzayi, but heaven knows how they communicated; most of the time they spent together was in joint meditation. Decoheim still couldn't speak, and the surroundings were so quiet that only the cries of seagulls could be heard.

At the age of six, Decoheim suddenly began to speak, which delighted Visera and Visera's "sister." By then, Weber had already shifted his focus to his younger son by the concubine, little Weber, and had long forgotten about Decoheim's affairs. But when he heard that Decoheim had suddenly started speaking, he was also pleased. However, the adults soon discovered that the language Decoheim spoke seemed incomprehensible to anyone. It was neither [[Truskean]] nor the southern barbarian language spoken by the Laprio people. A Jiaohuzayi from the temple who understood Glömezher was called to listen to Decoheim speak, and he also said it wasn't Glömezher. Visera was now both happy and shocked, her mind in turmoil. She returned to the mansion and focused on raising her daughter, who was less than a year old. She left Visera's "sister," Decoheim's "aunt," to specifically take care of Decoheim.

Being somewhat forgotten by his parents didn't seem to affect Decoheim's mood, but he went to the temple more and more frequently. His aunt couldn't really control him either, but when he went to the temple, he no longer wandered around or watched the monks' various rituals; he just meditated with the Jiaohuzayi. When the Jiaohuzayi read scriptures, he would watch silently beside them.

After about a year, he learned to write in ancient Glömezher script. One day, he tried writing a joke the monks told and brought it home to show Visera, who couldn't understand it. When he showed it to his aunt, she laughed heartily.

Probably a few more months passed. On the day before his seventh birthday, he saw water boiling on the stove in the side room, but the adults were all in the courtyard discussing something and didn't notice. In his anxiety, he shouted "Be careful!" in Glömezher. His shout, however, made the adults ignore the kettle and gather around him instead; his aunt rushed to him, wrapped him in her arms, and shed tears of excitement, saying in Glömezher, "My Decoheim can speak!", which made him embarrassed.

From then on, Decoheim gradually began to speak Glömezher. People thought perhaps he had spent too much time with the Jiaohuzayi and thus learned Glömezher. Many servants in the household privately called him "little Jiaohuzayi." Often, the servants could only understand half of what he said, so for most of the time, he only spoke with his aunt. When Weber heard about this, he was also very happy. He rushed back to Heimet from Laprio, had a good conversation with Decoheim in Glömezher, and was deeply shocked. That very night, he went to the Tanka that Decoheim frequented and talked with the abbot until midnight, then hurried back to Laprio by carriage at dawn.

From then on, Decoheim began attending another Tanka farther from home but closer to the Heimet imperial palace. There, specialized Jiaohuzayi guided Decoheim in reading scriptures, and several scholars who had previously served as imperial tutors at the court taught Truskean to the noble children nearby. It didn't take Decoheim long to master Truskean writing, but he still didn't speak much. Most of the time, he listened quietly to people talking, only expressing opinions when asked. Sometimes his Truskean sentence structure was strange, often imitated by his classmates in the school, but he didn't mind and still played with his classmates.

Decoheim's peaceful days didn't last long. After his Truskean became proficient, he started following his classmates in causing mischief—climbing roofs, catching birds, and killing snakes—which gave Visera and his aunt headaches. Weber, in his letters, told them to "let him be." Not long after, Decoheim quieted down again, watching the monks practice martial arts, but lost interest after a few days.

As the things he could do in the Tanka gradually decreased, for some reason he began studying shipbuilding. He pulled together a few friends he met at the Tanka and plotted to cross the Heimet Strait to see the other side. Unfortunately, they encountered a great storm. One of the children drowned in the strait, and another became terrified of the sea.

Decoheim was ten years old this year. Coinciding with Weber's return to Heimet, he was grounded for three months. After three months, Weber was about to depart for the eastern part of the empire. Before leaving, he seemed somewhat concerned about Decoheim and asked him what he wanted to do. Decoheim couldn't say clearly. So Weber sent him to the palace to serve as an attendant under the Imperial Guard. Serving as an attendant was actually a common tactic for nobles to foster relationships between their children and potential heirs to the throne, nothing particularly unusual among the Heimet upper class. Called an Imperial Guard attendant, it was more like a social arena for a privileged class. The eldest prince had died young, and the second prince Hetuke's place was overcrowded, so Decoheim ended up in the third prince Aset's side palace guard. He quickly became friends with the officers under Prince Aset.

Aset had favored martial arts since childhood, participating in military training within the palace alongside the Imperial Guard from a young age. His martial talent wasn't one in a million, but it was outstanding. The Imperial Guard instructor once commented that Aset was "like a sea serpent." Compared to Aset, Hetuke had almost no accomplishment in martial arts but was highly skilled in the Tanka Sect's Spirit Language, which was extremely popular in Trusk at the time. This made Aset particularly jealous. The Tanka Sect's Spirit Language was a form of "magic" highly dependent on innate talent, making it difficult for Aset to surpass Hetuke through later effort. After learning about Decoheim's nickname "little Jiaohuzayi," Aset developed a different attitude towards Decoheim. Although Decoheim at that time actually knew little about Spirit Language, Spirit Language users themselves were very rare, and any extraordinary phenomenon would be interpreted as a manifestation of Spirit Language.

The time in the palace was a peaceful and happy period for Decoheim. Daily drills with the Imperial Guard strengthened his physique and gave him some understanding of the organization of small-scale combat units. In the palace library, he also enriched his knowledge of Spirit Language. Trusk典籍 did not have many records on Spirit Language; instead, some books in the palace written in Glömezher contained detailed records of Spirit Language. However, Decoheim's experiments based on the典籍 never succeeded. Aset still encouraged Decoheim to continue his experiments—he needed a helper accomplished in Spirit Language to counterbalance Second Prince Hetuke in future power struggles. Aset and Decoheim thus formed a deep friendship.

However, Hetuke was also quite interested in Decoheim, often discussing topics related to Spirit Language with him in the palace's scripture repository. During this period, the palace's Jiaohuzayi, Moswarike's Ludimart, was their most frequently contacted Spirit Language mentor. The Spirit Language in the court was mainly focused on the Fanhai Sect's prophecies. Decoheim learned some, gaining a glimpse into the "realm" of Spirit Language.

During his years in the palace, Decoheim witnessed both the glory and splendor of the royal family and their various acts of debauchery, immorality, and mutual strife. He deeply felt the underlying crises within the imperial rule, but as a youth, he did not pay much attention to it.

Days seemed to pass peacefully, until one day news of a plague arrived from the southern part of the empire. At first, it was just a town, a village, then it spread to the entire Laprio, then to the whole of Trusk, and even as far north as Mistnatila was not spared. The plague took the emperor's life and, one evening, also took Decoheim's mother, plunging him into deep grief. At this time, his father was missing, and only Decoheim and his aunt remained in the Heimet home. Even more unfortunately, the southern Laprio people used the plague as a pretext to rebel again. Decoheim was conscripted by the Imperial Navy and had to part with his aunt. It was from this moment that Decoheim's legendary story began.

docs.title: The Empire's Turmoil
docs.text:
The empire's internal strife began during this terrible epidemic. Decoheim was conscripted by the Imperial Navy, serving as a ship captain's adjutant—an officer assisting the captain with daily life, food, and conveying orders—on the imperial patrol vessel Andonmat. The ship's captain was Ludimart from Jaudzya, hailing from a family with generations of military service.

With the Imperial Navy, Decoheim traveled to many places. During this time, he met his first love—a Laprio girl from the south named Shegvi. He was captured by Shegvi and her tribe while performing a shore mission, intended to be exchanged with the fleet for supplies. But when they returned, they found their tribal territory had been occupied by people from another tribe, with the entire tribe either dead or fled. They were also pursued by enemies while fleeing their territory, suffering heavy casualties. In the end, only Shegvi and Decoheim remained. The two entered a temple and discovered prophecies about the plague inside. Decoheim set traps, cooperating with Shegvi to injure the pursuers. Shegvi was injured protecting Decoheim during the fight, losing an eye.

Decoheim brought her back to the ship and tried his best to treat her, but found that many crew members strongly objected to treating a barbarian who had kidnapped other crew members and taken many supplies. Some even had ill intentions towards Shegvi. Therefore, once Shegvi showed slight improvement, Decoheim took her ashore to search for Shegvi's scattered tribe members. He encountered obstruction from the captain but to no avail.

Finally, Decoheim found Shegvi's tribe members. Shegvi's uncle was dissatisfied with him, but at Shegvi's pleading, he agreed to give Decoheim a chance to join the tribe. After much thought, Decoheim ultimately decided to return to the ship temporarily and planned to arrange a time to meet Shegvi again.

However, before Decoheim and Shegvi could successfully arrange a meeting, he was attacked by assassins who had been lurking nearby. In the chaos, Decoheim was separated from the tribe, his fate unknown. He struggled with all his might to escape and finally encountered the captain and his party, who had come ashore looking for him after receiving news of the assassins. Decoheim returned to the ship again, his heart filled with concern for Shegvi and their unfinished arrangement.

Although Decoheim's actions should have been considered desertion, due to his noble status, the captain did not court-martial him but instead placed him under temporary house arrest. Although it was temporary house arrest, Decoheim's days were actually much the same as before—still following the captain around—which also drew considerable criticism from the crew. Although the ship stayed on the Novaya for long periods, only approaching the coast when supplies were needed, the plague eventually caught up with the ship. Half the crew fell ill, and Decoheim also fell ill. During his illness, Decoheim saw a light and believed it was a revelation from the God Gyua. After Decoheim recovered from his illness, he seemed to have gained the ability to "cure" this plague. His prestige in the military grew rapidly.

The captain soon became critically ill from the plague. With no news from the empire for a long time, the ship fell into internal strife. One faction proposed defecting from the empire to become free people on the sea—essentially pirates, whom the Imperial Navy had long been fighting. Another faction insisted on returning to Heimet Port under the Royal Navy flag, despite the risk of pirate attacks. Although Decoheim had considerable prestige in the military at this time, he, still young, refused to lead either faction and was unwilling to side with any particular group. This ultimately led to mutual slaughter among the crew; the plague also claimed many crew members' lives. In the end, less than a quarter of the original crew remained. The captain eventually survived the plague, but continuous high fever left him with some disabilities. Under the captain's guidance, Decoheim finally began learning to shoulder leadership responsibilities and ultimately stabilized the situation. They stopped at Bollasom Port to recruit some crew, defeated several pirates at sea, incorporated some ships, and finally returned to Heimet Port. However, Heimet was now heavily besieged by rebel forces.

Decoheim took his trusted followers through a secret passage into Hashite City to search for his aunt, but to no avail. Then they managed to infiltrate the palace, discovered the besieged Hetuke, and discussed the current situation with him. Hetuke requested Decoheim's help in activating an ancient ritual from the old books to call for reinforcements from the garrison in the Trusk Basin to lift the siege of the imperial city. After much thought, Decoheim decided to help Hetuke. The ritual ultimately failed. Coincidentally, Decoheim discovered Aset imprisoned by Hetuke at this time, rescued Aset, and escaped from Hashite City. From Aset, he learned that the emperor had actually passed the throne to Aset on his deathbed, but Hetuke, unwilling to accept this, colluded with the capital guard and placed Aset under house arrest.

Thus, Decoheim decided to go with Aset to the Trusk Basin to try to find reinforcements to quell the rebels everywhere and counterbalance Hetuke,顺便 searching for his father and aunt along the way. Passing through the Geziwa region, they were attacked by several bands of bandits, one of which was led by his father's guard captain, Jaudzya's Presp. From him, Decoheim learned that his father was not in the Trusk Basin but had gone south at the beginning of the plague to try to stabilize the Laprio situation. He had originally arranged for Presp to escort Visera (i.e., his aunt) back to Heimet, but they also encountered bandits on the way and were separated from Visera in the chaotic fighting. However, Presp was quite sure Visera was alive but did not explain the reason in detail to Decoheim. Decoheim was puzzled but, considering the tense situation, did not press

docs.title: The Heimet Family
docs.text: The Heimet Family is a powerful noble family that has repeatedly appeared throughout the history of Tusk. Its rise is closely linked to the prosperity of commerce and trade. During the late period of the Quileo Dynasty, the city of Hashet became a significant commercial center due to its advantageous port conditions, which allowed the Heimet family to amass substantial economic power.

docs.title: Notable Figures
docs.text: Name Brief Description Mataxosamen I An outstanding military strategist and politician in the history of Tusk, considered the de facto founder of the Heimet Dynasty Mataxosamen II The first monarch of the Heimet Dynasty

docs.title: 马塔肖萨门Matasioksamem
docs.text: Matasioksamem is a proper noun in ancient Truskean. Although it became relatively rare during the Middle Truskean period, it was commonly used as a personal or clan name in ancient times. Furthermore, in modern Truskean contexts, this term often refers to the famous Matasioksamem Avenue.

docs.title: Etymology and Meaning
docs.text:
Linguists speculate that the word "Matasioksamem" belongs to a transitional stage from Northeastern Laaerad to ancient Truskean. Its semantic construction consists of two parts:

  1. Prefix mata-:

    It is considered a dialectal narrative prefix. It may be similar to the proto-language suffix -mat, but during its evolution, this morpheme inverted its position and moved to the beginning of the word.

  2. Root sioksamem:

    Meaning "broad mountain" or "continuous mountain range".

In summary, the literal meaning of this name is roughly "people living in the mountains" or "people from the mountains".

docs.title: Grammatical Declension
docs.text:
When "Matasioksamem" is used to refer to geographical features (such as roads), it usually follows specific grammatical rules:

  • Definite suffix: Generally, the *-ir* suffix is added for modification.

  • Noun combination: Often used in conjunction with *lous* (road) or *dzal lous* (avenue).

    • Example: Matasioksamem-ir dzal lous (Matasioksamem Avenue).

docs.title: Historical Research
docs.text:
Based on the etymological meaning ("people from the mountains"), historians have proposed a hypothesis regarding the origin of the Heimet family:

  • Geographical Origin: The Matasioksamem father and son likely originated from the Selk Mountains, the mountain range closest to the Heimet region, which aligns with the description of "mountain people".

  • Surname Misattribution: Given the descriptive nature of the term, "Matasioksamem" most likely was not originally the personal name of these monarchs, but rather their surname or clan name. However, over the long course of historical change and oral transmission, their true names have been lost. Later generations then mistakenly transmitted this clan name as their personal names, while the core region they ultimately controlled, Heimet, became their surname.

docs.title: Historical Figures
docs.text: Entry Identity and Brief Description [[Matasioksamem I]] The first documented patriarch of the Heimet family, the founder. Matasioksamem II Son of Matasioksamem I, an important early ruler of the Heimet dynasty. Matasioksamem III A famous tyrant and conqueror, a mid-period monarch of the Heimet dynasty.

docs.title: Geographical Features
docs.text: Entry Brief Description Matasioksamem Avenue An important strategic road connecting the core Heimet region, initially constructed during the reign of Matasioksamem I.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Heimet: Several core figures of this family bear the name Matasioksamem.

  • Heimet Dynasty: The dynasty initiated by the Matasioksamem father and son.

docs.title: Matasioksaamenmatasioksaamen I
docs.text: Matasioksaamen I (Truskean: matasioksaamen), also known as Matasioksaamen of Heimet, was an outstanding military strategist and statesman in ancient Truskean history, considered the de facto founder of the Heimet Dynasty. He overthrew the corrupt Quileo Dynasty and laid the foundation for Truskean commercial and military expansion through a series of reforms.

docs.title: Life
docs.text:
Matasioksaamen was born in the northern port city of Heimet in the ancient Truskean region. During the late Quileo Dynasty, Trusk fell into turmoil due to prolonged drought and internal court strife. Seizing the opportunity, Matasioksaamen raised an army in the name of the Sea God. While the southern Laprio people invaded amidst the chaos and the main force of the Quileo army was pinned down, he led his troops to swiftly capture Trusk's core cities, successfully cutting off the Quileo army's food and supply lines, ultimately causing its collapse.

He did not directly ascend to the throne but instead captured the young Quileo king, taking control of the government into his own hands. To consolidate his rule, he quelled rebellions across the land and eventually married his son, Matasioksaamen II, to a princess of the Quileo family, legally inheriting the throne as a son-in-law, formally inaugurating the Heimet Dynasty.

docs.title: Reforms of Matasioksaamen
docs.text: During his reign, Matasioksaamen implemented a series of reforms. These measures not only stabilized the situation but also laid the groundwork for the future development of the Trusk Empire:

docs.title: Religious Reform
docs.text: Matasioksaamen reorganized the mythological systems of the various city-states, elevated the status of the Heimet Sea God within the pantheon, and constructed a unified Heimet-Trusk belief system to integrate the people's ideology.

docs.title: Tax Reform
docs.text: Matasioksaamen improved the tax laws, attempting to levy taxes on merchants traveling between city-states, providing the state with a stable source of revenue.

docs.title: Legal Development
docs.text: Matasioksaamen attempted to implement some basic legal statutes. Although a codified law was ultimately not formed, it provided a reference for the later development of the legal system.

docs.title: Infrastructure Development
docs.text: Matasioksaamen constructed the famous Matasioksaamen Road, connecting the Heimet Strait with the Trusk Basin, greatly facilitating the exchange of information and goods. Furthermore, he built ports along the coastal areas, creating conditions for Trusk's territorial expansion to the west of the strait.

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text: Matasioksaamen I was a ruler of great foresight and ambition. Through military means and political skill, he successfully ended the chaotic situation of the Quileo Dynasty. Through a series of reforms, he transformed index from an alliance of inland city-states into a maritime empire with strong commercial and military potential. Although he himself never formally declared himself emperor, his rule and reform measures are considered a crucial turning point in Truskean history, ushering in the era of commercial prosperity known as the Heimet Dynasty.

docs.title: Related Entries
docs.text: Entry Relation Heimet Family Matasioksaamen was a member of the Heimet family. Matasioksaamen Road One of Matasioksaamen's major achievements. Matasioksaamen II Son of Matasioksaamen.

docs.title: Matasioksaamenmatasioksaamen II
docs.text: Son of [[Matasioksaamen I]], the first monarch of the Heimet First Dynasty. During his reign, he continued his father's policies, developed the empire's economy, and further consolidated the political system of the Tusk core region and the ruling legitimacy of the Heimet family. He is a rather important monarch in Tusk history.

docs.title: Biography
docs.text:
Year of birth unknown.

In 883 BC, he assassinated the sole male heir of the Quileo family, then seized power as the son-in-law of the Quileo family. By winter, he usurped the throne and ascended.

During his reign, he continued most of his father's policies, further advancing the reform of the Tusk state's administrative system and implementing a bureaucratic system locally. He constructed the Port of Borlasom in the gulf between Turha and Heimet.

Died in 870 BC, cause of death unknown.
