docs.title: Laaeradlaaerad Basic Setting
docs.text: Laaerad is a unique world that skillfully blends hard science fiction settings with traditional fantasy elements. It not only possesses a mysterious magical ring, but its magic system is not凭空 generated; it is based on a complex cosmological theory. In this world, historical processes and civilizational development are closely intertwined with astronomical phenomena and magical events, collectively shaping a universe full of unknowns and exploration.

docs.title: 1 Astronomy
docs.text: From an astronomical perspective, Laaerad is a planet with mass and volume extremely similar to Earth, with comparable gravity and climatic conditions, thus nurturing diverse ecosystems and intelligent life. It orbits its own star and is accompanied by a satellite; the structure of its star system resembles that of the Solar System. However, the most prominent feature of this planet is a shimmering Magical Ring that orbits it in a unique way. The ring is not only a beautiful night sky spectacle but also an important indicator of the effective strength of "magic" in the world. When it appears in the night sky, it reflects sunlight to illuminate the heavens, most of the time as bright as the moon, but sometimes appearing purplish-red or grayish-green; during the day, depending on the relative position of the ring and the sun, from specific angles, people on the ground can even see two suns simultaneously in the sky—the spectacle of "two suns sharing the sky."

docs.title: 2 Magic
docs.text:
The "magic" of Laaerad follows the rules below:

Beyond "reality," there exists a higher-dimensional space. The real world is mapped within it in the form of "snapshots." This mapping is unique and reversible, containing not only all spatial information but also encompassing the flow of time. The occurrence of magic is essentially the caster opening a channel between reality and a certain "snapshot," causing the current state of reality to collapse towards that "snapshot." During the collapse, energy, waves, and matter are exchanged between the two. Periodically, when the astronomical-scale "reality" rotates to a position close to a certain mapping location in the higher-dimensional space, the Magical Ring becomes visible. Based on the aforementioned magic mechanism, during this period, most magic fails. This cycle can be extremely long, spanning hundreds of years, or as short as multiple repetitions within a single day.

docs.title: 2.1 Forms
docs.text:
Magic manifests in various forms in Laaerad:

  • Enchantment (uru) By binding "elements" to the higher-dimensional mapping of an item, the item gains abilities beyond its physical properties. For example, an enchanted sword can cut non-physical substances when swung, and enchanted clothing can isolate extreme temperature differences. The stability of an enchantment depends on the caster's mastery of the purity and compatibility of the elements. Once the binding process becomes unbalanced, the item may fail or even backfire on its holder.

  • Blessing (anhuz) By using a magical channel, an ideal state from a "snapshot" is superimposed onto a target, thereby enhancing its vitality, luck, or mental state. Blessings are commonly used in rituals such as pre-battle ceremonies, weddings, and harvest festivals. The effects are usually not significant and it is also a common courtesy; many peoples of Laaerad have folklore related to "blessing."

  • Prophecy (portesdra) A rare and extremely difficult form to master, it involves selectively reading information from "snapshots" to predict the possible course of events. However, "snapshots" exist in a higher-dimensional space and are not fixed. What the caster sees may be chaotic and模糊 impressions, and the reading process can cause mental strain, with severe cases potentially losing perception of reality.

  • Spiritualism (strahuy) Some Spirit Speakers or Mages who have previously ventured into the higher-dimensional space may leave their imprints there. By exploring these imprints, a Spirit Speaker practicing spiritualism may obtain some ideas from predecessors (or possibly successors).

Regardless of the form, the casting of magic relies on the acquisition of specific "elements"—these elements could be matter, energy, symbols, or environmental conditions. The key to stably repeating spellcasting lies in whether the caster can accurately find and acquire the required elements in each attempt.

docs.title: 2.2 Mechanism
docs.text:
In Laaerad, so-called "Mages" are those who can see the "snapshots" in the higher-dimensional space. A caster's strength largely depends on their ability to observe "snapshots." However, this talent for observing the higher-dimensional space is extremely rare—it only has a probability of manifesting in newborn infants when the mapping of the higher-dimensional space coincides with the real world to some extent. Although it seems to be heritable through bloodlines, due to insufficient samples, no stable pattern has been summarized to date.

Those who can observe "snapshots" do not necessarily possess the ability to precisely locate elements; those who can locate elements may not be able to observe enough elements. Even those proficient in both may lose their spellcasting ability if they cannot open a channel between reality and the higher-dimensional space. It is known that meditation helps strengthen the connection with the higher-dimensional space, but the effect varies from person to person.

Taking adding fire to a sword as an example:

  1. In reality, the sword exists as a physical entity;
  2. The mage first glimpses and analyzes its "snapshot," then attempts to open a channel;
  3. In the "snapshot," the state is static and unchanging. The caster must find the elements constituting fire, bind them to the sword's mapping, and manifest the fire into reality through the channel.

On the surface, this is no different from the magic forms in typical fantasy worlds. However, in this setting, the effect and strength of magic depend on the relative position of the real universe and the "snapshot." Therefore, by observing the stars, people can predict the feasibility and power variations of magic during a certain period; in specific timeframes, magic may even completely fail. Consequently, the world of Laaerad may harbor highly developed magical civilizations, as well as social structures that completely lack magical technology.

This system provides a self-consistent physical and astronomical foundation for magic while leaving ample room for science fiction-style creation.

Inspiration source: Asimov's The Gods Themselves

docs.title: 3.1 History
docs.text:
%%{init: { 'logLevel': 'debug', 'theme': 'dark' } }%%
timeline
    title Laaerad Timeline
    section Mythological Age
        BC 20 : Records of Primitive Laaerad Language
    section Cataclysmic Age
        Early : Glacial Migration
        : Human Groups Disperse
        Late : Geological Activity Stabilizes
        : Primitive Settlements Appear
    section Medieval Age
        Early : City-State Era
        Late : Early Imperial Era

docs.title: 3.1.1.1 Overview
docs.text:
Under warm and stable climatic conditions, Laaerad's sea levels were high, with extensive shallow seas covering the continental margins, resulting in relatively limited land area. Terrestrial flora and fauna were diverse, and the climate was suitable, allowing early humans and other intelligent races to proliferate and spread in temperate and coastal regions.

Humans appeared approximately one hundred thousand years before the end of the Mythological Age and gradually spread across the temperate zones and coastlines of the continent. Twenty years before the end of the Age, the Primitive Laaerad Language was first recorded in stone carvings—about two hundred words engraved on rock, becoming the oldest extant linguistic document. During this period, myths of the Porurka gods were prevalent, with language, tribes, and nature worship highly intertwined, forming the spiritual core of early society.

At the end of the Age, a figure historians call the "Door Opener" discovered a "Snapshot Door" leading to an early state of the universe. The opening of this door caused a massive amount of water vapor to instantly freeze and fall back onto the planet, leading to a sharp drop in sea levels, abrupt climate change, and intense crustal movements. Old continental seabeds were exposed in many places, and mountains rose. This disaster marked the end of the Mythological Age and the beginning of the Cataclysmic Age.

docs.title: 3.1.2.1 Overview
docs.text:
The Cataclysmic Age lasted about a thousand years, a period of intense geological and climatic fluctuations. Polar ice caps expanded rapidly, permafrost zones formed extensively, sea levels continuously dropped, and some inland lakes dried up. Plate tectonics and volcanic eruptions were frequent, shaping new mountains and rifts.

The harsh climate forced human groups to migrate on a large scale, crossing ice fields and land bridges to disperse across the continents. The Primitive Laaerad Language rapidly diverged into various dialects and early independent languages due to prolonged isolation. The glacial migration waves promoted cultural diversification and regional isolation. Stable hunting and gathering centers emerged in some temperate regions, establishing the first fixed camps.

By the end of the Age, geological activity slowed, the climate gradually warmed, glaciers began to retreat, and low-lying areas were re-submerged by seawater. The development of primitive settlements in various regions laid the foundation for agriculture and handicrafts, ushering in a new civilizational process.

docs.title: 3.1.3 Medieval Ageyupoyur
docs.text:
As the climate stabilized, regions across the continent entered a production stage centered on agriculture and animal husbandry. Food surpluses drove population growth and urban expansion. Regional civilizations emerged successively, with limited mutual交流, and most cultures evolved independently.

Bronze smelting technology gradually became widespread and transitioned towards the Iron Age. Metal tools and weapons significantly enhanced warfare and engineering capabilities. Some regions rediscovered and mastered magic, triggering the rise of "secondary civilizations."

This period saw the emergence of early city-states built around river valleys, lakes, and bays. Regional trade and resource competition gave rise to inter-city-state alliances and hegemonic structures. By the late Age, early imperial雏形 appeared. A few civilizations established跨区域 rule through military conquest and cultural assimilation, laying the groundwork for the formation of subsequent great empires.

docs.text:

Entering the early Medieval Age, the Primitive Language gradually diverged under the influence of geographical isolation and交流.

Northeastern Laaerad Language evolved into the Northeastern Language Family, giving birth to three classical languages: Old Glömezher, Laprio, and Old Truskean. Old Glömezher retained many ancient grammatical structures and was influenced by Old Truskean. Laprio was distributed in coastal areas, absorbing elements from Truskean and overseas languages early on. Old Truskean developed into Middle Truskean, becoming the core language for official and written communication. The Old Northern Language in the north, although sharing a common origin with the Northeastern Language Family, diverged early, and some of its dialects became indirect ancestors of Old Glömezher.

The languages of the Yiyin, Loos, and Kuotai language families, distributed in the central and southwestern parts of the continent, directly inherited from the Primitive Language, with less external influence, retaining more archaic features in their phonology. Language evolution was influenced not only by geographical isolation but also by ethnic migration, contact, and融合. Languages within the Northeastern Language Family渗透 through mutual influence, while relatively isolated branches preserved the ancient面貌 of prehistoric languages, providing important clues for后世 research.

docs.title: MistnatilaMistnatila
docs.text: Mistnatila is a special administrative region and major geographical area in the northeastern part of the Trusk Empire, and also the core territory of Glömezher (Glömer) civilization. As the empire's nominal autonomous kingdom and a strategic barrier in the north, it is not only the empire's most important warhorse breeding ground but also a hub connecting the North Sea shipping routes with the inland economy.

docs.title: Etymology and Semantics
docs.text:
In the ancient context of the Glömezher language, "Mistnatila" is not merely a geographical reference but a condensed mythological prayer, meaning "the azure forests and snow-capped mountains in the embrace of the Merciful Goddess." This name precisely deconstructs the region's topographical features and spiritual beliefs:

The prefix Mist specifically refers to the deep azure green of coniferous forests in the ancient language, alluding to the vast taiga forest sea covering the northern part of the region; the middle element Nati means gray-white, which is the etymological root for "snow-capped mountains" in Glömezher, directly pointing to the perpetually snow-covered, rocky mountain range in the west; and the suffix La at the end is a phonetic variation of the ancient ra, referring to Ramro, the wife of the Giant King in Glömezher mythology, known as "Merciful Heart."

docs.title: Geography and Ecology
docs.text:
The topographical skeleton of Mistnatila is supported by the Mistnatila Mountains in the west. This majestic barrier features towering snow peaks, perpetually ice-bound and treacherous to traverse. It not only blocks the frigid air currents from the northern wastelands but also serves as a natural boundary between this region and the empire's western inland areas. The mountains contain high-quality iron ore and associated silver deposits, but extraction is extremely difficult due to the harsh, frigid climate.

East of the mountains, the terrain gradually descends into open alluvial plains and hills, known as the famous Glömezher Grasslands. Moderated by ocean currents from the East and North Seas, the climate here is relatively mild, exhibiting the distinct Mist (azure green) characteristic. The north is covered by dense boreal coniferous forests, rich in precious furs like sable and silver fox; the south transitions into lush temperate meadows, the primary breeding ground for the famous Kuotai warhorses and long-haired yaks. Haida Port on the eastern coast is the region's only deep-water harbor and serves as the homeport for the empire's North Sea Fleet and a terminus for trade.

docs.title: History and Political Evolution
docs.text:
In the early days of the Trusk Empire, this area was known as Xinxinazhi, a corrupted borrowing of the Glömezher word for "north" in Truskean, and was an arena for competition among scattered Glömezher clans. Gu Zhuoba Gu, the founder of the Moswarike dynasty, briefly unified the various tribes here, forming an early political prototype. After the collapse of the Heimet Second Dynasty, the region broke away from southern control for a time, until the founder of the Tyesuku dynasty, De Zhuohai I, implemented the grand strategy of "Pacify the North, Campaign in the South."

In that turbulent era, De Zhuohai did not opt for forceful military conquest of this vast frozen land. Instead, through political marriage and recognition of the hereditary rights of local nobles (remnants of the Moswarike family and later the Bötes Zhuaohu family), he secured the submission of the Glömezher people. In the third year of the imperial calendar, when the empire established new administrative divisions, Mistnatila was formally enfeoffed as a Kingdom-level Administrative Region.

Today, Mistnatila plays the role of the "Shield of the North" within the empire. Although nominally autonomous under local nobility, preserving unique nomadic customs and spiritual language beliefs, the empire constructed a massive military stronghold at the southern choke point of the mountain range—Sanmen (Mountain Gate). This fortress serves both as a key defensive point against northern disturbances and as an outpost for the imperial center to monitor this autonomous kingdom. Under this delicate balance, a steady stream of warhorses, ores, and furs flows south through Sanmen, supporting the empire's military equipment and economy.

docs.title: Trusktrusk🏛️
docs.text: The term "Trusk" refers both to the historical Trusk Empire and, more broadly, to the language, culture, and ethnic groups of the region. Compared to other regions, Trusk is characterized by its tradition of centralized authority, strict military systems, and cultural uniformity.

docs.title: Etymology
docs.text: "Trusk" originates from the Strait Truskean word "trusk", with an earlier root in the proto-language "tʰro", meaning "to make" or "to forge". This likely reflects the region's early technological advantages in shipbuilding and metalworking.

docs.title: Related Concepts
docs.text:
• Ancient Trusk: In a narrow sense, refers to the city-state period before the Kuileot Dynasty; broadly, it can refer to the collective name for the various city-states and tribes within the Trusk Basin.
  • Trusk Basin: The ancient Trusk region, the cradle of Trusk civilization.
  • Trusk Kingdom: The embryonic form and early legal tradition of the empire, which later split into the Heimet and Tyesuku kingdoms, each inheriting parts of its institutions and culture.

docs.title: Trusk Region 🌍
docs.text: The Trusk Region is an independent geographical unit in the central part of the eastern continent of Laaerad. It is bordered by the Novaya Sea to the north, the Anioksait rainforest to the east, and is surrounded by the Tyesuku Mountains to the west and the Guduka-Asesa Mountains to the south. This enclosed yet internally connected topography made it the birthplace of Trusk civilization and a natural hub connecting the continent's north-south and east-west routes.

docs.title: Geographical Boundaries 🗺️
docs.text:
The natural boundaries of the Trusk Region are clear, forming a "mountains on three sides, sea on one side" pattern, creating a geostrategic advantage of being easy to defend:

  • Northern Boundary: Follows the southern coast of the Novaya Sea and the Heimet Strait, with a rugged coastline providing channels for external communication.
  • Western Boundary: The Tyesuku Mountains act as a barrier, blocking desert winds and sands from the west. This mountain range is also an important mineral distribution zone.
  • Southern Boundary: Separated from the Laprio region by the Guduka-Asesa Mountains, with only a few mountain passes available for travel.
  • Eastern Boundary: East of the lower Per'nan River delta, gradually transitioning to the edge of the Anioksait rainforest, forming a climatic and vegetative transitional zone.

docs.title: Topography ⛰️
docs.text: The regional topography can be summarized as "one basin, one river, one coast, two walls".

docs.title: Trusk Central Basin
docs.text: As the main body of the region, this is a vast plain formed by the long-term alluvial deposits of the Per'nan River and its tributaries. It features flat terrain, fertile, slightly alkaline soil, and is known as the "Empire's Granary". It is one of the most densely populated and agriculturally developed areas on the eastern continent.

docs.title: Per'nan River System 🌊
docs.text: Originating in the Guduka Mountains, it flows from south to north through the basin, eventually emptying into the Novaya Sea. The river has abundant water, a gentle flow, and significant seasonal water level changes. Its middle and lower reaches are navigable year-round, serving as a crucial waterway connecting northern resources and ports.

docs.title: Heimet Coast
docs.text: A subsidence-type coast with a rugged, indented shoreline featuring numerous capes and bays, forming several natural deep-water harbors. The Heimet Peninsula on the western side guards the strategic throat of the strait.

docs.title: Eastern and Western Transition Zones
docs.text:
• Western Highlands: Hills and plateaus extending from the Tyesuku Mountains into the basin, mostly used for pasture and orchards.
  • Southern Piedmont: The northern foothills of the Guduka Mountains, characterized by fragmented terrain and numerous canyons, forming a natural defensive line against the south.

docs.title: Climate Characteristics ☀️🌧️
docs.text:
Trusk has a Novaya Sea-type climate (similar to a Mediterranean climate), with precipitation and heat occurring in different seasons:

  • Summer (Dry Season): Controlled by continental high pressure, hot and dry, conducive to the ripening of crops like wheat and grapes.
  • Winter (Rainy Season): Influenced by maritime westerlies, mild and rainy, with unfrozen rivers, suitable for winter wheat growth.
  • Local Variations: The western mountains receive more precipitation, with snow in winter; the east, influenced by rainforest humidity, is more humid in summer with frequent thunderstorms.

docs.title: Mineral Resources
docs.text:
• Ferrous Metals: The Tyesuku area contains high-grade magnetite and hematite deposits, easy to mine, providing raw materials for "Trusk steel".
  • Precious Metals: The southern Ruilian area is rich in gold deposits, with associated silver deposits in the west.
  • Stone: The basin edges are rich in limestone and marble, used for construction and building materials.

docs.title: Biological Resources
docs.text:
• Agriculture: Mainly produces durum wheat, barley, grapes, olives, and figs.
  • Forestry: The mountainous areas have abundant ironwood and oak, providing timber and charcoal for shipbuilding and smelting.
  • Fisheries: The Novaya Sea continental shelf is rich in fishery resources, famous for cod, tuna, and the "Heimet Purple Shell".

docs.title: Related Entries 🔗

[[Truskean Language]] Language The official language of the empire, influencing the formation of many subsequent dialects and derived languages. [[Trusk Chronicle|Trusk Chronicle]] History Records major historical events and political-social changes of the empire and its territories. [[Trusk Mythology]] Culture A collection of myths and legends reflecting the empire's belief and cultural systems. [[Trusk Map]] Geography A map marking the empire's territory, important cities, waterways, and topography.

docs.title: Andon
docs.text: The concept of Andon, within the context of the Trusk Empire, refers both to the mountainous region in the northwestern part of the empire (i.e., the Andon territory) and to the main ethnic group inhabiting this area. Andon and its people have played a significant role in the history of the Trusk Empire, particularly in military conflicts and border relations. Their unique geographical environment and way of life have shaped their formidable national character.

docs.title: Anioksait
docs.text: Anioksait is a vast tropical rainforest region on the eastern continent of Laaerad, forming the natural eastern boundary of the Trusk Empire.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: Anioksait is renowned for its dense tropical rainforest ecosystem and serves as a natural barrier to the eastward territorial expansion of the Trusk Empire. Its unique geographical environment has played a significant role in the empire's history, limiting its further expansion.

docs.title: Geographical Features
docs.text: Anioksait is located on the eastern border of the Trusk Empire and is an extensive tropical rainforest. Its hot, humid climate and dense vegetation make the area difficult to traverse, forming a natural geographical obstacle.

docs.title: Historical Significance
docs.text: In the history of the Trusk Empire, the Anioksait Jungle marked the limit of the empire's westward border expansion. During the Heimet Dynasty period, despite the empire's continuous efforts to expand its territory eastward, it was ultimately halted by the Anioksait Jungle, as the army could not advance further. This rainforest thus became a natural limit to the empire's territory, indirectly influencing its strategic layout and developmental direction.

docs.title: Related Entries
docs.text: Entry Relation Trusk Empire Anioksait is the eastern boundary of the Trusk Empire Heimet Dynasty Anioksait once limited the westward expansion of the Heimet Dynasty

docs.title: Novaya Sea
docs.text: The Novaya Sea is a vast inland sea in the eastern continent of Laaerad, playing a pivotal role in the history, geography, economy, and culture of the Trusk Empire.

docs.title: Geographical Features
docs.text:
The Novaya Sea is located in the eastern part of the Trusk Empire's territory. Its coastline boasts the continent's most fertile arable land, forming extensive plains highly suitable for agriculture. It connects to the North Sea and the South Sea via the Heimet Strait and the Laprio Strait, controlling the most crucial transportation hub on the continent.

  • Climate: The core region of the Novaya Sea primarily experiences a climate where rainfall and heat occur in different seasons. However, the northern part is also influenced by North Sea ocean currents, resulting in some areas with a temperate maritime climate.
  • Resources: The Novaya Sea is rich in fishery resources, with a great variety and abundance of fish, along with various other marine products.

docs.title: Strategic and Economic Importance
docs.text:
The geographical location of the Novaya Sea makes it the cornerstone of the Trusk Empire's commercial and military hegemony:

  • Transportation Hub: The Heimet Strait, as the only strait in the Novaya Sea with a deep-water port unaffected by ocean currents and never freezing, serves as a crucial hub for the Trusk Empire's advance into and retreat from the Novaya Sea.
  • Commercial Lifeline: Since the First Heimet Dynasty, the shipping and trade system of the Novaya Sea has been the primary economic source for the various Trusk dynasties. Favorable climatic conditions, the advanced shipbuilding skills of artisans, and the "magic"-enhanced sealed longships provided many conveniences for the commercial activities of the index. Goods from across the empire, including sable furs from the northern frontier.
  • Agricultural Foundation: The fertile coastal farmland provides the empire with a stable food supply, serving as a crucial guarantee for its population and economic development.

docs.title: Cultural Influence
docs.text:
The Novaya Sea has had a profound impact on Trusk culture:

  • Spiritual Symbol: The flourishing commercial activities fostered a spirit of freedom and exploration among the Trusk people.
  • Religious Association: There are many folk festivals related to navigation, closely associated with the Sea God faith. As the core indigenous religion of Trusk, the Sea God faith's belief system is intimately connected to the ocean, viewing the sea as a symbol of the world's primordial chaos, fate, and the natural cycle.
